Title: Data Scientist
Description:
R0001536 Data Scientist (Open)

We’re Nadara. We work harmoniously with the power of nature and the communities around us to power lifetimes to come. We call our approach ‘living energy’.

We don’t just produce renewable energy, we live it – recognising our relationship with the people touched by our business and supporting social, educational, cultural, and environmental initiatives that contribute to the development of the communities we work alongside.

Discover more about our business here: https://nadara.com/about/

Nadara is an innovative place to work. We work in a stimulating and challenging environment, where every day we explore the unknown with curiosity, make decisions with quality and take action and deliver with courage. For us diversity is a real value, and we encourage in connecting different perspectives with respect.

Discover more about our culture and approach here: https://nadara.com/living-the-company/

Job Description Summary

Per la società Energy Team siamo alla ricerca di un(a) Data Scientist da inserire nel team R&D - Data Modelling and Analtycs.

La risorsa sarà coinvolta nello sviluppo di progetti di Data Science e dedicata allo sviluppo di soluzioni innovative nell'ambito dell'efficienza energetica e della digitalizzazione

Job Description

I progetti includeranno:


Utilizzo LLM: sviluppo di pipeline end-to-end tramite Agentic AI, orchestrazione LLM e integrazione nei sistemi aziendali
Attività di analisi dati di processi industriali utilizzando modelli di data analytics e machine learning (es. studio time series, sviluppo digital twin)
Analisi di simulazione e ottimizzazione: supporto alle aree aziendali per migliorare l'operatività e facilitare decisioni strategiche
Collaborazione allo sviluppo di funzionalità collegate alla piattaforma di analisi energetica CloE
Creazione di applicazioni di data visualization: sviluppo di dashboard interattive e report dinamici


Profilo richiesto


Buona capacità di programmazione unita a competenze statistiche matematiche.
Laurea in una delle seguenti discipline: data science, fisica, matematica, statistica, ingegneria matematica, ingegneria energetica, ingegneria fisica, scienze informatiche.
Uso avanzato di Python con un focus sulle applicazioni di data mining, machine learning e framework LLM (es. Langchain)
Conoscenza avanzata di metodi statistici, tecniche di machine learning e LLM e loro applicazione
Forte orientamento all’apprendimento continuo specialmente in ambito scientifico
Conoscenza Microsoft Office


Sono Requisiti Graditi


Conoscenza linguaggi interrogazione dati (SQL, API Rest)
Familiarità sviluppo microservizi
Dimestichezza strumenti di versionamento (GitHub) e gestione di ambienti virtuali (venv)
Esperienza nell’uso dei seguenti pacchetti/framework Python: scikit-learn, LangChain, LangGraph, TensorFlow, PyTorch, Dash, Quarto
Competenze in ambito energetico, di impianti industriali e di produzione da fonte rinnovabile
Buona conoscenza lingua inglese


Inquadramento e retribuzione: Contratto a tempo indeterminato, CCNL Commercio, Salary Range: 30-40k.

Location:

Trezzano sul Naviglio

Time Type

Full time

Worker Subtype

Regular

Title: Data Scientist
Description:
📍 Sede: Milano (ibrido) | 🕐 Contratto a tempo indeterminato | 🍽 Ticket Restaurant + Welfare

MCA Groupe, multinazionale francese di consulenza ingegneristica ed informatica, è nata a Parigi nel 1991. Oggi è presente in 7 Paesi in Europa: Italia, Francia, Belgio, Germania, Olanda, Svezia e Spagna.

Con oltre 1500 talenti nel mondo e una cultura orientata alla crescita, ci posizioniamo come partner strategico per i principali player in settori ad alto contenuto tecnologico.

MCA Italia, con sedi a Torino, Milano e Roma, è attiva nei settori Mobility & Digital e conta oltre 190 professionisti.

🎯 Siamo alla ricerca di un Data Scientist & Machine Learning Engineer

La figura sarà inserita nell’area Data di un cliente, contribuendo allo sviluppo di modelli predittivi, sistemi di raccomandazione e soluzioni di ottimizzazione per supportare decisioni e strategie data-driven. Lavorerà su progetti innovativi ad alto impatto, collaborando con team multidisciplinari per trasformare dati complessi in soluzioni concrete a supporto del business.

💻 L’attività prevede:


Definire le variabili decisionali, gli obiettivi, i vincoli, i parametri e le ipotesi alla base del modello.
Analizzare la qualità, la completezza e l'idoneità dei dati rispetto agli obiettivi di modellazione.
Sviluppare, sperimentare e affinare modelli di ottimizzazione, algoritmi euristici e approcci ibridi.
Verificare, insieme alle funzioni di business e operative, la fattibilità e l'effettiva applicabilità delle soluzioni proposte.
Documentare i limiti del modello, i principali trade-off, le strategie di fallback e le condizioni di utilizzo.


⚡ Competenze tecniche:


Esperienza nello sviluppo di modelli di Machine Learning applicati a casi d’uso reali
Ottima conoscenza di Python e delle principali librerie di Data Science e Machine Learning;
Esperienza nella realizzazione di modelli di forecasting e predictive analytics;
Conoscenza di LP, MILP, Constraint Programming, euristiche e problemi combinatori.
Competenze in statistica, modellazione matematica e analisi quantitativa;
Esperienza con algoritmi di ottimizzazione come OR-Tools, Pyomo, PuLP, Gurobi, CPLEX, SCIP o simili.


🎯 Il profilo che cerchiamo:


Esperienza di almeno 2-3 anni in ambito Data Science o Machine Learning;
Laurea in Informatica, Ingegneria, Matematica, Statistica, Fisica o discipline affini;
Approccio analitico e data-driven;
Capacità di lavorare in team e di interfacciarsi con stakeholder tecnici e business.


🤝 Quello che ti offriamo:


Per profili con almeno 2 anni di esperienza è prevista una retribuzione iniziale a partire da €30.000, commisurata alle competenze tecniche e all’esperienza maturata, con un percorso di crescita strutturato.
Per profili con una seniority superiore, la proposta retributiva verrà definita nel corso dell’iter di selezione, sulla base dell’esperienza, delle competenze e del nostro Career Path. La progressione economica avviene secondo i livelli previsti dal CCNL.
Ticket buoni pasto da 10€ e Welfare basato sulla seniority aziendale
Un Career Path definito attraverso criteri oggettivi e verificabili, che ti accompagnerà nella crescita professionale in MCA Italia
Ambiente internazionale e cultura aziendale orientata all'innovazione e alla collaborazione


💡 Non ti riconosci in tutti i requisiti?

In MCA Italia crediamo nel potenziale delle persone e nella crescita continua. Se il ruolo ti interessa e pensi di poter portare valore con le tue competenze e la tua motivazione, ti invitiamo comunque a candidarti, anche se non rispecchi al 100% i requisiti indicati.

⚖️ Pari Opportunità, Privacy e Trasparenza

Promuoviamo una cultura aziendale fondata sul rispetto, sull’inclusione e sulla valorizzazione delle persone. Nel processo di selezione le candidature sono valutate esclusivamente sulla base di competenze, esperienze e attitudini professionali, senza alcuna discriminazione, diretta o indiretta, legata a genere (L. 903/77 e L. 125/91), età, origine etnica, orientamento sessuale o altre caratteristiche non rilevanti ai fini della valutazione.

I dati personali raccolti sono trattati con riservatezza e nel rispetto della normativa vigente in materia di protezione dei dati personali, del Regolamento (UE) 2016/679, delle procedure interne e del Codice Etico aziendale.

Le persone candidate sono invitate a consultare l’Informativa Privacy, ai sensi degli artt. 13 e 14 del GDPR, disponibile al seguente link:

https://mca-website.cdn.prismic.io/mca-website/aCG1jSdWJ-7kR6kA_MGR-INF-RM002-ENGDataProtectionPolicy.pdf

L’annuncio è rivolto anche a candidat* appartenenti alle categorie protette ai sensi degli artt. 1 e 18 della L. 68/99.

Title: Data Scientist - Fraud Detection
Description:
Are you looking for a new challenge?

Fancy helping us shape the future of motor insurance?

Prima could be the place for you.

Since 2015, we’ve been using our love of data and tech to rethink motor insurance and bring drivers a great experience at a great price. Our story began in Italy, where we’ve quickly become the number one online motor insurance provider. In fact, we’re trusted by over 5 million drivers. And now we’re expanding to help millions more drivers in the UK and Spain.

To help fuel that growth, we need a Data Scientist to join our Anti Fraud Team.

In this role, you’ll have the opportunity to tackle fraud, one of the most impactful and fast-evolving challenges in insurance. You will do this from a global perspective. In fact, the team is responsible for detecting fraudulent claims across all Prima markets: the UK, Spain and Italy.

You’ll collaborate with engineers, claims handlers and business leaders to improve the detection of fraud, directly influencing real-world fraud decisions with impact on the company’s bottom line measured in millions. This is a highly practical area of work where strong business judgment is just as important as technical expertise.

Excited to make an impact? Here are the details

What You’ll Do


Design and improve fraud detection systems that prioritize high-risk claims, optimizing the trade-off between detection accuracy and operational efficiency
Collaborate across teams and functions, working closely with fraud analysts, engineers, and product managers
Develop deep expertise in fraud dynamics, identifying emerging fraud patterns, understanding how they evolve across the insurance lifecycle, and translating these insights into effective detection and prevention strategies, for example identifying suspicious networks of related claims, synthetic entities, or anomalous behavioral patterns


What We’re Looking For


Extensive experience (3+ years) in data science or applied machine learning, leading end-to-end initiatives in complex technical domains and delivering measurable business impact
Strong analytical and quantitative mindset able to translate data into insights, assess model performance, and guide decision-making with evidence and clarity
Structured problem solver skilled at framing ambiguous challenges, defining success metrics, and communicating results to both technical and non-technical audiences
Strong Python programming skills, especially in the data domain, and fluent use of Git
Knowledge of Spark, SQL or any other data-oriented programming language and proficiency with standard data wrangling and modeling libraries (Pandas, Sklearn, Keras, XGBoost, LightGBM, etc.)


Nice-to-Have


Experience in analytical fraud detection in the Insurance, Finance or Law Enforcement domain
In-depth knowledge of graph databases (Neo4j, AWS Neptune) and analytics (NetworkX, GNN) or explainability applied to anomaly detection
Knowledge of BI tools (Tableau or similar)


Why you’ll love it here

We want to make Prima a happy and empowering place to work. So if you decide to join us, you can expect plenty of perks.

🤸 Work Your Way: Enjoy hybrid working, with a mix of home and office days. Plus, for up to 30 days per year, work from anywhere.

🏁 Grow with us: We may move fast at Prima, but we move together. Get access to learning resources, mentorship and a growth plan tailored to you.

🌈 Thrive and perform: Your best work begins when you feel your best. Enjoy private healthcare, gym discounts, wellbeing programs and mental health support.

Think you’re a match? Apply now.

At Prima, we celebrate uniqueness. If you don’t meet every requirement but are passionate about this role, we still want to hear from you. Innovation thrives on diverse perspectives.

Prima is proud to be an equal opportunity employer. Need accommodations during the process? Email us at accessible.recruiting@prima.it. Let’s build the future of insurance, together.

Title: Data Scientist
HTML:
PTE consulting è una società di consulenza aziendale che accompagna i propri clienti nel loro percorso di Digital Transformation, sviluppando soluzioni di business e sistemi IT per migliorare i processi aziendali nelle aree AFC, Treasury, Supply Chain e Data Intelligence.

Lavoriamo con importanti clienti internazionali guidandoli nell'innovazione dei processi aziendali, progettando nuove soluzioni che consentano di gestire le prossime sfide tecnologiche e raggiungere il successo.

Per il potenziamento della nostra area di competenza Data & Analytics siamo alla ricerca di Data Scientist con almeno 3 anni di esperienza nell'analisi avanzata dei dati e nello sviluppo di modelli predittivi e machine learning in ambiente Databricks.

La figura inserita sarà coinvolta in progetti per clienti internazionali - operanti principalmente nel settore industriale - e lavorerà all'interno di team strutturati, occupandosi di attività progettuali legate alla gestione, trasformazione e valorizzazione dei dati.

In particolare, si occuperà di:

  • analisi di dati complessi provenienti da diverse sorgenti;
  • sviluppo di modelli statistici, predittivi e di machine learning;
  • identificazione di insight a supporto delle decisioni di business;
  • attività di data preparation, data cleaning e feature engineering;
  • validazione, monitoraggio e ottimizzazione dei modelli sviluppati;
  • utilizzo di linguaggi e strumenti per l'analisi dei dati, come Python, SQL e principali librerie di data science;
  • collaborazione con team funzionali e tecnici per tradurre le esigenze di business in soluzioni analitiche;
  • redazione di report, dashboard e documentazione tecnica a supporto delle attività progettuali.

Requirements

La ricerca è rivolta a profili professionali con esperienza in ambito Data Science, maturata su progetti di analisi avanzata, modellazione statistica, machine learning o sviluppo di soluzioni data-driven.

La candidatura ideale ha sviluppato una buona conoscenza delle principali tecniche di analisi dati, modellazione predittiva e machine learning, nonché competenze nell'utilizzo di Python e SQL per l'analisi, la trasformazione e la valorizzazione dei dati. Costituisce elemento di interesse la familiarità con ambienti cloud/data platform, in particolare Databricks, PySpark e strumenti a supporto del ciclo di vita dei modelli, come MLflow. È inoltre apprezzata la capacità di sviluppare dashboard, strumenti di reporting e analisi a supporto dei processi decisionali.

È richiesto il possesso di una laurea in discipline STEM, una buona padronanza della lingua inglese e la capacità di relazionarsi con interlocutori tecnici e funzionali.

Oltre alle competenze tecniche, cerchiamo colleghi che condividano il nostro approccio e modo di lavorare: persone motivate e orientate al lavoro in team, con capacità di iniziativa e voglia di contribuire all'identificazione di soluzioni innovative, che desiderano partecipare al successo di una società in espansione che offre ampi spazi di crescita professionale.

Benefits

In aggiunta alla retribuzione fissa sono previsti una componente bonus (variabile in base a risultati aziendali e performance individuali), buoni pasto, assicurazione sanitaria aziendale, percorsi di formazione e certificazioni finanziati dall'azienda.

Lavoriamo prevalentemente in smart working/da remoto, con sedi di riferimento a Milano, Roma o Pescara.

Ti troverai in un ambiente giovane e collaborativo, che promuove una cultura che valorizza diversità, equità e inclusione.

Title: GROWTH DATA SCIENTIST
Description:
WHAT IS EDENRED?




Edenred is a global leader in the employee benefits sector, offering companies digital solutions dedicated to improve the well-being of people at work.




A digital platform that connects 60 million users and over 2 million affiliated businesses in 44 countries through about 1 million corporate clients. In Italy, the ecosystem involves 3 million users, over 180.000 corporate clients and more than 300.000 affiliated establishments, making its network the most widespread, present in every Italian country.




Edenred’s solutions improve quality of life and strengthen employee engagement—from lunch breaks to leisure time, personal care, and sustainable mobility. Meal vouchers, shopping vouchers and corporate welfare tools are tailored to all types of organizations and employees. They support HR departments, make companies more attractive and efficient, and stimulate both the labor market and the local economy.




True to the Group’s purpose “Enrich connections. For good.”, these solutions develop and enrich connections that empower businesses and promote inclusive solutions, placing people, the environment, and local communities at the center through a concrete ESG commitment.




Edenred’s 12,000 employees are dedicated every day to making the world of work a more connected, safer, more efficient, and more responsible ecosystem.




In 2024, thanks to its global technology assets, the Group managed close to €45 billion in business volume, primarily carried out via mobile applications, online platforms and cards.




Edenred is listed on the Euronext Paris stock exchange and included in the following indices: CAC Large 60, Euronext 100, Euronext Tech Leaders, FTSE4Good, DJSI Europe Index, DJSI World Index, and MSCI Europe.




YOU WILL VIBE WITH US




We are looking for a Growth Data Scientist with proven experience in developing Advanced Analytics and Machine Learning solutions to support business processes, particularly in the areas of Sales and Customer Management.

The successful candidate will work closely with business stakeholders, transforming data and insights into concrete actions while contributing to the adoption of models within operational processes.

This role requires a strong combination of technical expertise and business acumen, operating in a hybrid environment that combines model development with their practical application across business functions.




Key Responsibilities:




Design, develop, and deploy Machine Learning and Advanced Analytics models
Build predictive models (e.g., churn, cross-sell, up-sell, propensity models, etc.) and develop the related features and datasets
Translate models into operational outputs (customer lists, scores, targeting logics) to support commercial processes
Collaborate with business stakeholders to understand requirements and translate them into data-driven solutions
Monitor model performance and business impact, contributing to a test-and-learn approach
Present insights, results, and strategic recommendations to management
Work closely with Data Engineering and IT teams to ensure data quality, governance, and availability
Contribute to the evolution of the company’s data-driven strategy




WE WILL VIBE WITH YOU




Passion for customer, respect, imagination, simplicity and entrepreneurial spirit are the values we look for in our people. If you recognize yourself and want to get involved in a stimulating and multinational environment, send us your application and become an Edenreder too!




Minimum 3–5 years of experience in Data Scientist roles, preferably within Sales or Customer Analytics
Strong knowledge of Python, PySpark, SQL, and major Machine Learning libraries (e.g., scikit-learn, XGBoost, TensorFlow, or similar)
Hands-on experience with Azure Databricks (including Workflows and Job Orchestration) and familiarity with the Azure ecosystem: Azure Machine Learning, Azure DevOps, Azure Data Factory
Practical experience in developing predictive models with end-to-end project ownership (e.g., propensity, churn, segmentation)
Ability to work with complex and/or incomplete datasets
Good understanding of data architectures and ETL/ELT pipelines for data preparation and transformation
Degree in a scientific or quantitative discipline
Fluent English




Soft Skills & Qualifications




Strong interest in applying analytical models to real business contexts (particularly Sales and Marketing, though not exclusively)
Excellent stakeholder management and communication skills, including interaction with senior management
Strong storytelling and presentation skills for both technical and non-technical audiences
Ability to interpret data strategically and with a results-oriented mindset
Ability to translate models and analyses into actionable business outputs




Nice to Have




Experience with Data Visualization tools (Power BI or similar)
Experience in CRM and campaign management environments
Familiarity with dbt, Airflow, and development environments such us VS Code




OTHER DETAILS




Permanent Contract
CCNL Commercio
Gross Annual Salary (RAL) starting from € 38.000, determined on the basis of objective and transparent criteria, including professional experience gained, specialized technical skills, the level of autonomy and responsibility required, and alignment with the company's compensation and job classification framework
Performance bonus in line with the company's incentive plan







🏡 Smart-working (10 days/month)
🍽️ Ticket Restaurant®
🛍️ Welfare Plan
🩺 Comprehensive health benefits, including supplementary medical insurance, accident protection and life coverage







EQUAL OPPORTUNITIES AND HIRING PROCESS




Edenred fosters an inclusive workplace grounded in equal opportunity, with recruitment and career development processes based on fairness, transparency, and merit.

Candidates are assessed without discrimination on the basis of gender, age, ethnicity, social background, nationality, sexual orientation, personal or religious beliefs, disability, or any other legally protected characteristic.

Title: Data Scientist
Description:
Are you looking for a new challenge?

Fancy helping us shape the future of motor insurance?

Prima could be the place for you.

Since 2015, we’ve been using our love of data and tech to rethink motor insurance and bring drivers a great experience at a great price. Our story began in Italy, where we’ve quickly become the number one online motor insurance provider. In fact, we’re trusted by over 5 million drivers. And now we’re expanding to help millions more drivers in the UK and Spain.

To help fuel that growth, we’re looking for Data Scientists to join our Pricing & Underwriting Department, the strategic engine behind Prima's growth.

As part of this global team, you’ll play a pivotal role in shaping our strategy and strengthening our competitive edge. Using data, analytical thinking, machine learning and business acumen, you’ll help predict the future and set the right prices, keeping our offering both attractive and sustainable. You’ll work with your data science tools and skills to bring real-world impact, collaborating with cross-functional teams to turn insights into action and fuel innovation across the company and its different branches.

Excited to make an impact? Here are the details.

What You’ll Do


Leverage data to identify risks, opportunities and key pricing drivers, turning complex information into actionable insights that influence strategic decision making;
Leverage Machine Learning and advanced analytics to develop risk and pricing models that power real-time insurance pricing for millions of customers;
Contribute directly to the company’s growth and long-term success through innovative modelling, experimentation and commercially impactful insights.


What We’re Looking For


Strong academic background in Mathematics, Physics, Engineering (Maths, Physics and Computer Science specialisations), Data Science, Computer Science or Statistics;
Strong quantitative, logical and analytical abilities, a solid foundation in statistics, probability and advanced analytics, and a curious, proactive and innovative mindset focused on solving complex problems;
Fluency in English, both spoken and written.


Nice-to-Have


Strong communication skills, with the ability to challenge assumptions, and drive new ideas;
Possess a solid foundation in code development and machine learning, ideally in Python;
Know how to create, manipulate and analyse large datasets.


Why you'll love it here

We want to make Prima a happy and empowering place to work. So if you decide to join us, you can expect plenty of perks.

💵Competitive compensation: Benefit from competitive salaries, starting from €45k up to €75k, with clear opportunities to grow as you do.

🤸 Work Your Way: Enjoy full flexibility – work from home, the office or a mix of both. Plus, work from anywhere for up to 30 days a year.

🏁 Grow with us: We may move fast at Prima, but we move together. Get access to learning resources, mentorship and a growth plan tailored to you.

🌈 Thrive and perform: Your best work begins when you feel your best. Enjoy private healthcare, gym discounts, wellbeing programs and mental health support.

Think you’re a match? Apply now.

At Prima, we celebrate uniqueness. If you don’t meet every requirement but are passionate about this role, we still want to hear from you. Innovation thrives on diverse perspectives.

Prima is proud to be an equal opportunity employer. Need accommodations during the process? Email us at accessible.recruiting@prima.it. Let’s build the future of insurance, together.

Title: AI & Data Science Engineer
Description:
Job Description

DXC Technology is looking for Data Scientist to support in AI and Generative AI projects, contributing to practical solutions and improving business processes through ML models, NLP and intelligent automation.

Key Responsibilities


Support the design and implementation of AI solutions, including machine learning models, NLP, and computer vision applications.
Collaborate with senior team members to understand client requirements and translate them into practical AI solutions.
Assist in presales activities, including preparing technical materials and contributing to client presentations.
Stay up-to-date on AI trends and emerging technologies, applying knowledge to support solution development.
Participate in small-scale AI solution projects or tasks, gaining hands-on experience in real client environments.


Requirements


2-5 years of experience in Gen AI projects.
Good knowledge of machine learning frameworks and libraries (Python, TensorFlow, PyTorch, Scikit-learn, etc.).
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills.
Good communication skills and ability to work in a collaborative team environment.
Ability to translate business requirements into practical AI solutions, including design and implementation.


Nice To Have


Experience with Agentic AI approaches or platforms.
Previous involvement in presales or solution support tasks.


Location: Milano (Cernusco) with smart working possibility

Why Join Us  

Offer Package  

The position offers a gross annual base salary starting from €48,000. 

Compensation is determined in line with the applicable collective labor agreement (CBA: Contratto Collettivo Nazionale di Lavoro per l’industria metalmeccanica e della installazione di impianti) and based on objective and gender-neutral criteria, including responsability, experience and skills. 


Health Insurance 
Permanent disability by accident coverage 
Life and permanent disability by accident coverage 
Meal vouchers 
Supplemental Pension Plan  
Smart Working Agreement  
Employee Discounts Program  


Continuous Learning Access learning platforms and take part in skill development programs designed for you.  

Refer & Earn Help us find great talent through the Employee Referral Program and get rewarded for your contribution. 

Career Development DXC promotes professional growth and internal mobility by making every open position accessible to all employees. Through the Performance Development program, the company supports individual aspirations to achieve shared goals together. 

Our Way of Working At DXC, we believe that strong connections and collaboration drive success. Our hybrid work model combines flexibility with in-person teamwork — empowering you to perform at your best while maintaining balance and wellbeing. 

We’re proud to foster an inclusive, equitable and diverse workplace where everyone can thrive.

At DXC Technology, we believe strong connections and community are key to our success. Our work model prioritizes in-person collaboration while offering flexibility to support wellbeing, productivity, individual work styles, and life circumstances. We’re committed to fostering an inclusive environment where everyone can thrive.

Recruitment fraud is a scheme in which fictitious job opportunities are offered to job seekers typically through online services, such as false websites, or through unsolicited emails claiming to be from the company. These emails may request recipients to provide personal information or to make payments as part of their illegitimate recruiting process. DXC does not make offers of employment via social media networks and DXC never asks for any money or payments from applicants at any point in the recruitment process, nor ask a job seeker to purchase IT or other equipment on our behalf. More information on employment scams is available here.

Title: Data Science Generative AI Consultant
Description:
In Accenture, potrai portare la tua visione innovativa per reinventare interi settori di mercato, lavorando con le aziende più importanti a livello mondiale. Modella il futuro del business e della tecnologia sviluppando soluzioni all'avanguardia in grado di generare nuovo valore.

Il tuo ruolo e le tue prospettive

Entra a far parte del nostro network internazionale di talenti nel Reinvention Partner Industry & Enterprise in ambito AI and Data, all’interno del Centro di Eccellenza Advanced AI. Lavorerai con tecnologie innovative su progetti entusiasmanti, aiutando le aziende leader a livello mondiale a sviluppare le loro strategie IT. In particolare, potrai:



Analizzare i requisiti di business dei clienti e tradurli in soluzioni algoritmiche;

Sviluppare codice in Python utilizzando librerie e framework per la manipolazione e analisi dei dati;

Contribuire allo sviluppo di soluzioni basate prevalentemente su Generative AI e Large Language Models;

Gestire il ciclo di vita delle soluzioni dalla gestione e trasformazione dati tramite pipeline di ETL, sviluppo e validazione modelli (tramite definizione di test set, sfruttando librerie quali deepeval, ragas, quando possibile o codice custom) fino al deploy e monitoraggio in produzione su cloud a seconda dell’ambiente cliente;

Garantire qualità dei dati in ingresso e performance delle soluzioni sviluppate.



La retribuzione prevista in Accenture viene definita sulla base di molteplici elementi di valutazione, tra cui – ad esempio - il ruolo ricoperto, il livello di seniority, il grado di responsabilità, le competenze professionali e l’esperienza maturata. Per la presente posizione, è previsto un livello di inquadramento B3 nel CCNL Metalmeccanico

La fascia di retribuzione annua lorda prevista è compresa tra 36.906,87€ e 46.700€.

Se condividi la nostra passione per la consulenza in ambito Data & AI e hai le seguenti caratteristiche, ti stiamo aspettando

Required Qualifications



Esperienza professionale:

da 2 a 5 anni di esperienza nello sviluppo di implementazione di soluzioni AI;

Esperienza in modellazione e trasformazione del dato, strutture dati, sistemi di storage e partizionamento




Formazione:

Laurea magistrale in area STEM (Informatica, Ingegneria, Matematica, Fisica, Bioingegneria o simili).




Competenze tecniche:

Esperienza nello sviluppo in Python per analisi e manipolazione dati (es. Pandas, Polars, NumPy, Matplotlib);

Esperienza con tecniche di Machine Learning e Deep Learning;

Esperienza nell’utilizzo di Large Language Models;

Propensione all’uso di Coding Agents (e.g. Claude Code, Github Copilot, Codex).




Versioning e strumenti base:

Esperienza nell’utilizzo di strumenti di versioning del codice (Git);

Esperienza nell’utilizzo di database relazionali (SQL);

Esperienza almeno basilare di strumenti di containerizzazione (Docker). ️




Lingue:

Ottima capacità di lavorare in lingua italiana e inglese, sia scritta che parlata.


Preferred Qualifications



Esperienza o conoscenza di use case LLM quali:

Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG)

Summarization

Chatbot

Agents and MCP implementation


Policy

Qualora il tuo percorso di selezione andasse a buon fine, avrai la possibilità di aderire all’Accordo di smart working, ove compatibile con l’attività svolta, e lavorare da remoto secondo quanto previsto dalle policy aziendali. Per informazioni sulla nostra protezione della privacy art13 L.679/2016 (GDPR) vai su https://www.accenture.com/it-it/careers/privacy-policy

Accenture garantisce le pari opportunità.

Title: Data scientist
Description:
At Bending Spoons, we’re striving to build one of the all-time great companies. A company that serves a huge number of customers. A company where team members grow to their full potential. A company that functions at unparalleled levels of effectiveness and efficiency. A company that creates value for shareowners at an extraordinary rate. And a company that does so while adhering to high ethical standards.

In pursuit of this objective, we acquire and improve digital businesses, not to sell on, but to own and operate for the long term. The transformations we make are often deep—designed to speed up innovation, benefit customers, and strengthen business performance. Here, hierarchy is minimal and teams are small and talent-dense. We operate established products with the ambition, agility, and urgency of a startup. Across the company, we integrate AI deeply into how we work so that human judgment and machine intelligence reinforce each other.

For a talented, driven, and collaborative individual, working at Bending Spoons is an opportunity to learn, make an impact, and progress their career at an exceptionally high rate. That’s our promise to such a candidate.

A few examples of your responsibilities
Design and improve data pipelines. Contribute to the design and evolution of data systems—ensuring data is accurate, reliable, and efficiently processed across diverse tech stacks.
Ensure data correctness and scalability. Work closely with software engineers to validate data, define metrics, and improve the robustness of reporting and analytics systems.
Develop internal data products and tooling. Help build scalable solutions (e.g., reporting engines, forecasting tools) used by product teams across the company.
Support platform-wide decision-making. Partner with multiple teams to provide the analytical and modeling backbone for business planning and performance tracking.
Leverage AI to accelerate work and uncover insights. Use AI tools to move faster, surface ideas and opportunities, and study competing products and trends.


What we look for
Reasoning ability. Given the necessary knowledge, you can solve complex problems. You think from first principles, and structure your ideas sharply. You resist the influence of biases. You identify and take care of the details that matter.
Drive. You’re extremely ambitious in everything you do—and your initiative, effort, and tenacity match the intensity of your ambition. You feel deeply responsible for your work. You hold yourself to a high—and rising—bar.
Team spirit. You give generously and without the expectation of receiving in return. You support the best idea, not your idea. You're always happy to get your hands dirty to help your team. You’re reliable, honest, and transparent.
Proficiency in English. You read, write, and speak proficiently in English.


What we offer
Incredibly talented, entrepreneurial teams. You’ll work in small, result-oriented, autonomous teams alongside some of the brightest people in your field.
An exceptional opportunity for growth. We go to great lengths to hire individuals of outstanding potential—then, our priority is to put them in the ideal position to thrive. Spooners in their 20s lead products worth hundreds of millions of dollars. And if you’ve got what it takes, you’ll soon be playing an essential role in major projects, too.
All. These. Benefits. Flexible hours, remote working, unlimited backing for learning and training, top-of-the-market health insurance, a rich relocation package, generous parental support, and a yearly retreat to a stunning location. We help each Spooner set up the conditions to do their best work.
Competitive pay and access to company equity at a discounted price. Typically, we offer individuals with limited experience an annual salary of £85,797 in London and €66,065 elsewhere in Europe. For a candidate that we assess as possessing considerable relevant experience, the salary on offer tends to be between £112,189 and £250,512 in London, and €107,837 and €188,848 elsewhere in Europe. Compensation varies by location and expected impact, and grows rapidly as you gain experience and translate it into greater contributions. For individuals who demonstrate exceptional capability, we may offer compensation that extends beyond the usual ranges to reflect their higher expected impact. If you're offered a permanent contract, you'll also be able receive some of your pay in company equity at a discounted price, thus participating in the value creation we achieve together. If relocating to Italy, you may enjoy a 50% tax cut.


Commitment & contract
Permanent or fixed-term. Full-time or part-time.

Location
Milan (Italy), London (UK), Madrid (Spain), Warsaw (Poland), or fully remote from eligible countries.

The selection process
In our screening process, we prioritize verifiable signals of excellence, regardless of seniority. There are no preferential paths beyond what your application demonstrates, and we evaluate every candidate through the same process.

Some people hold back because they feel they lack experience or have an “imperfect” CV. If you like the role and believe you could excel over time, don’t self-reject.

All applications go through our careers page, which is the only way to be considered. If you pass our screening, you’ll be asked to complete one or more tests. They are challenging, may involve unfamiliar problems, and can take several hours. To learn more about what to expect throughout the selection process, you can find additional information here.

We set the bar high and won’t extend an offer until we’re confident we’ve found the right candidate. This is why a job may remain open for months or be reposted several times.

We consider all applicants for employment and provide reasonable accommodations for individuals with disabilities—please let us know through this form.

Before you apply
If you’ve applied before but didn't receive an offer, we recommend waiting at least one year before applying again.

Bending Spoons is a demanding environment. We’re extremely ambitious and we hold ourselves—and one another—to a high standard. While this tends to lead to extraordinary learning, achievement, and career growth, it also requires significant commitment.

To help you ramp up quickly and set yourself up for success, we expect you to spend most days in our Milan office during your first few months with us, regardless of your long-term work location. It’s the best way to rapidly absorb our company culture and build trust with your new teammates. We’ll support you with generous travel and accommodation assistance. After that, you’re welcome to work from one of our offices, or remotely from approved countries—depending on what we agree at the offer stage.

If the role speaks to you and you’re excited to give your best, we’d love to hear from you. Apply now—we can’t wait to meet you.

Title: Machine Learning Engineer
Description:
Job description:

Machine Learning Engineer
We are looking for a Machine Learning Engineer willing to join our Data Science and IoT Platform Team. The role involves building, training, deploying ML models, and collaborating on AI based applications to boost production efficiency and to enable predictive maintenance. It is also significantly involved in our digital transformation process and energy transition journey, offering the opportunity to influence them daily with creativity and innovative strength.
Key Responsibilities:
* Work across the end-to-end machine learning lifecycle, from problem definition and data preparation to model development, deployment, and monitoring
* Build, together with our Software engineers, AI based applications deployed on industrial machinery to boost production efficiency, reduce unplanned downtime and enable predictive maintenance
* Work with colleagues from different Product Lines to help them create and bring innovative solutions to the market

What we ask for:
* STEM Degree (Science, Technology, Engineering and Mathematics)
In depth experience with Python and knowledge of ML libraries (e.g. pandas, sklearn,
keras/ pytorch)
* Hands-on experience developing and deploying machine learning models

* Good Knowledge of English (written and spoken)

Nice to have
* Experience with building and deploying LLM models
* Experience with ML Computer Vision models
* Knowledge of Agile methodology and SCRUM
* Knowledge of Microsoft Azure. We use Synapse and ML Workspace

What we offer:
* The opportunity to contribute to Upstream&TAT - RAID activities with a direct impact on business performance
* A dynamic and international environment where ideas are valued and innovation is encouraged
* Continuous learning opportunities through training, exposure to new technologies and cross-functional collaboration.
* Hybrid working model according to company policy and role requirements
* Performance Management through an annual appraisal and development process
* Company Canteen available on site
* Supplementary healthcare coverage
* Well-being initiatives to support employees


For the position, the starting gross annual salary will be from € 35.000, with the final offer determined based on the candidate's seniority, experience, and skills, as well as the outcome of the selection process.
Workplace: Castellanza (VA)

Title: 数据科学家 ID209739
Description:
公司简介

Bending Spoons 是欧洲顶尖科技公司，旗下产品 Remini、WeTransfer、Evernote 等服务全球数亿用户。公司收购并深度优化数字产品，以创业魄力运营成熟业务，将 AI 全面融入工作流。层级极少，团队精干且人才密度极高。我们承诺才华与驱动力在这里有回报——极速学习、创造影响、实现跃迁。

核心职责

设计与优化数据管道。参与数据系统的设计与迭代，确保数据跨技术栈的准确性、可靠性与处理效率。
保障数据正确性与可扩展性。与软件工程师紧密协作，验证数据、定义指标、提升报表与分析系统的健壮性。
开发内部数据产品与工具。构建可规模化方案（如报表引擎、预测工具），服务各产品团队。
支撑全平台决策。与多团队协作，为业务规划与绩效追踪提供分析和建模支持。
借助 AI 加速工作并挖掘洞察。用 AI 工具提效、发现机会，并研究竞品与行业趋势。

任职要求

强逻辑推理。能从第一性原理解决复杂问题，不被惯性思维左右。
极强内驱力。主动性、投入度与韧性和雄心完全匹配，对自己的要求不断提高。
团队精神。乐于付出，支持最优方案而非"自己的方案"，值得信赖，坦诚透明。
英语能力。读写与口语流利。

福利待遇

顶尖人才密度。小而精、高度自治的团队，与业内最强者并肩。
稀缺成长机会。严苛招聘只为吸引高潜力人才，让你在最合适的位置快速成长；在这，20 多岁的 Spooner 们就在主导价值数亿美元的产品——有实力，机会随之而来。
有竞争力薪资与股权。伦敦£85,797起，资深可达£250,512；欧洲其他地区€66,065起，可达€188,848。薪资随贡献快速增长，实力者不设上限；可转股权；搬迁意大利享所得税减半。
福利应有尽有。灵活工作时间、远程办公、全额学习和培训、顶级医疗保险、优厚育儿假、办公零食饮料畅取、年度团队旅行。
完善签证与搬迁支持。全程签证与居留许可协助、搬迁补贴、机票报销、临时住宿及医疗保险，覆盖直系家属。

工作地

米兰/伦敦/马德里/华沙

工作及面试说明

我们重视可验证的卓越信号，非资历——简历不完美也请勇于申请；我们平等考虑所有候选人，残障人士可通过此表单申请合理便利；
招聘流程：线上投递——测试——多轮面试——发放offer；
入职时间灵活，支持毕业前兼职起步；前期望驻米兰办公，快速融入文化与团队，公司提供住宿、差旅补贴；后续可选择办公室或获批国家远程办公；
前申请者建议间隔一年再申。

Title: AI & Data intelligence Research Engineer
Description:
Per progetti innovativi in ambito Data Analytics, Artificial Intelligence e Machine Learning, siamo alla ricerca di un/una AI & Data intelligence Research Engineer da inserire all’interno di team multidisciplinari impegnati nell’analisi e valorizzazione dei dati.

La risorsa si occuperà di elaborare, analizzare e interpretare grandi volumi di dati attraverso tecniche statistiche avanzate e modelli di Machine Learning, con l’obiettivo di estrarre insight utili e generare valore per il business e per i clienti.

Attività principali


Analizzare e processare dati strutturati e non strutturati provenienti da fonti eterogenee
Sviluppare modelli statistici e algoritmi di Machine Learning e Deep Learning
Identificare pattern, trend e opportunità attraverso tecniche avanzate di analisi dei dati
Realizzare dashboard e strumenti di visualizzazione a supporto dei processi decisionali
Collaborare con team tecnici e stakeholder di business per trasformare i requisiti in soluzioni data-driven
Contribuire a progetti di innovazione e ricerca applicata in ambito Data Science e Artificial Intelligence


Requisiti


PhD o Laurea Magistrale in Data Science, Ricerca Operativa, Ingegneria Informatica, Informatica o discipline affini
Ottima conoscenza del linguaggio di programmazione Python
Gradita conoscenza del linguaggio Java
Esperienza nell’utilizzo dei principali toolkit e librerie di Data Science e Machine Learning
Esperienza con framework di Deep Learning quali PyTorch e TensorFlow
Conoscenza delle principali architetture e tecniche di AI Generativa, inclusi Prompt Engineering, Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) e Vector Database
Conoscenza dei database relazionali e del linguaggio SQL
Conoscenza dei principali strumenti di Data Visualization, tra cui Plotly.js, D3.js, ggplot, Tableau e QlikView
Solide competenze nell’analisi e interpretazione dei dati mediante tecniche statistiche e modelli predittivi
Passione per la Data Exploration, l’analisi dei dati e la realizzazione di modelli a supporto dei processi decisionali
Approccio quantitativo, spiccate capacità analitiche e orientamento al problem solving
Buona conoscenza della lingua inglese, scritta e parlata


Cosa ti offriamo?


Contratto di assunzione a Tempo indeterminato con applicazione del CCNL Metalmeccanico Industria;
Per questa posizione è prevista una fascia retributiva pari a € 29.000 - € 38.000 lordi annui in coerenza con il CCNL applicato, il livello di inquadramento previsto delle griglie di riferimento per il ruolo professionale proposto. Il posizionamento all’interno della fascia sarà determinato sulla base di criteri oggettivi e neutrali rispetto al genere, tra cui competenze tecnico-professionali, competenze comportamentali e organizzative, ed elementi qualificanti del profilo rispetto ai requisiti della posizione, quali esperienze professionali rilevanti, certificazioni, conoscenze specialistiche o ulteriori elementi professionali pertinenti.
Ticket Restaurant 7.50€
Rimborso mezzi di trasporto (Area urbana)
Smart Working
Welcome Kit: smartphone, Pc e zaino Spindox
Formazione tecnica e manageriale


“L’offerta è rivolta a candidati di entrambi i sessi (L.903/77 – L.125/91). Spindox promuove un ambiente di lavoro inclusivo e assicura pari opportunità di impiego a tutte le persone, indipendentemente da genere, età, nazionalità, cultura, orientamento personale o credo.”

HTML:
Per progetti innovativi in ambito Data Analytics, Artificial Intelligence e Machine Learning, siamo alla ricerca di un/una AI & Data intelligence Research Engineer da inserire all’interno di team multidisciplinari impegnati nell’analisi e valorizzazione dei dati.

La risorsa si occuperà di elaborare, analizzare e interpretare grandi volumi di dati attraverso tecniche statistiche avanzate e modelli di Machine Learning, con l’obiettivo di estrarre insight utili e generare valore per il business e per i clienti.

Attività principali

  • Analizzare e processare dati strutturati e non strutturati provenienti da fonti eterogenee
  • Sviluppare modelli statistici e algoritmi di Machine Learning e Deep Learning
  • Identificare pattern, trend e opportunità attraverso tecniche avanzate di analisi dei dati
  • Realizzare dashboard e strumenti di visualizzazione a supporto dei processi decisionali
  • Collaborare con team tecnici e stakeholder di business per trasformare i requisiti in soluzioni data-driven
  • Contribuire a progetti di innovazione e ricerca applicata in ambito Data Science e Artificial Intelligence

Requisiti

  • PhD o Laurea Magistrale in Data Science, Ricerca Operativa, Ingegneria Informatica, Informatica o discipline affini
  • Ottima conoscenza del linguaggio di programmazione Python
  • Gradita conoscenza del linguaggio Java
  • Esperienza nell’utilizzo dei principali toolkit e librerie di Data Science e Machine Learning
  • Esperienza con framework di Deep Learning quali PyTorch e TensorFlow
  • Conoscenza delle principali architetture e tecniche di AI Generativa, inclusi Prompt Engineering, Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) e Vector Database
  • Conoscenza dei database relazionali e del linguaggio SQL
  • Conoscenza dei principali strumenti di Data Visualization, tra cui Plotly.js, D3.js, ggplot, Tableau e QlikView
  • Solide competenze nell’analisi e interpretazione dei dati mediante tecniche statistiche e modelli predittivi
  • Passione per la Data Exploration, l’analisi dei dati e la realizzazione di modelli a supporto dei processi decisionali
  • Approccio quantitativo, spiccate capacità analitiche e orientamento al problem solving
  • Buona conoscenza della lingua inglese, scritta e parlata

Cosa ti offriamo?

  • Contratto di assunzione a Tempo indeterminato con applicazione del CCNL Metalmeccanico Industria;
  • Per questa posizione è prevista una fascia retributiva pari a € 29.000 - € 38.000 lordi annui in coerenza con il CCNL applicato, il livello di inquadramento previsto delle griglie di riferimento per il ruolo professionale proposto. Il posizionamento all’interno della fascia sarà determinato sulla base di criteri oggettivi e neutrali rispetto al genere, tra cui competenze tecnico-professionali, competenze comportamentali e organizzative, ed elementi qualificanti del profilo rispetto ai requisiti della posizione, quali esperienze professionali rilevanti, certificazioni, conoscenze specialistiche o ulteriori elementi professionali pertinenti.
  • Ticket Restaurant 7.50€
  • Rimborso mezzi di trasporto (Area urbana)
  • Smart Working
  • Welcome Kit: smartphone, Pc e zaino Spindox
  • Formazione tecnica e manageriale

“L’offerta è rivolta a candidati di entrambi i sessi (L.903/77 – L.125/91). Spindox promuove un ambiente di lavoro inclusivo e assicura pari opportunità di impiego a tutte le persone, indipendentemente da genere, età, nazionalità, cultura, orientamento personale o credo.”

Title: AI Scientist
Description:
CHI SIAMO

A partire dal 1° gennaio 2026 Fastweb S.p.A. e Vodafone Italia S.p.A., a seguito della fusione per incorporazione deliberata dalle rispettive assemblee degli azionisti, diventano un’unica realtà societaria sotto la denominazione di Fastweb S.p.A.

Il progressivo processo di integrazione tra le due società, avviato sin da gennaio 2025, rappresenta un’opportunità straordinaria per continuare a offrire a famiglie, imprese e pubbliche amministrazioni servizi sempre più avanzati e soluzioni tecnologiche all’avanguardia facendo leva sulle connessioni fisse e mobili in 5G più performanti del Paese, l’IoT, il Cloud e l’Intelligenza Artificiale e mantenendo al centro la qualità e l’affidabilità che da sempre contraddistinguono Fastweb e Vodafone Italia.

Cosa troverai in Fastweb?

I nostri valori: Spark Passion, Aim High, Stand True orientano il nostro lavoro quotidiano

Insieme creiamo un ambiente dove ogni persona può fare la differenza

We feel close #BELONGING

Creiamo insieme un ambiente di lavoro informale e accogliente, in un clima orientato alla collaborazione e al rispetto.

Mostriamo apertura e curiosità per incoraggiare idee innovative e generare un impatto tangibile per noi e per i nostri clienti.

We are smart #FLEXIBILITY

Il lavoro per obiettivi, lo smartworking, l’autonomia e la fiducia sono principi chiave della nostra cultura organizzativa.

Ci poniamo sfide ambiziose e le affrontiamo insieme con coraggio e responsabilità.

We learn #IMPACT

Coltiviamo il nostro talento, favorendo la crescita e il cambiamento.

Ispiriamo le persone a essere protagoniste del proprio sviluppo, grazie a programmi innovativi e a percorsi professionalizzanti.

We feel good #WELLBEING

Le persone sono la nostra priorità.

C’è grande attenzione al benessere fisico e psicologico, garantendo un ambiente sicuro e positivo e un sano work life balance.

We are unique #INCLUSION

L’inclusione è una scelta che rende l’ambiente creativo, dinamico e ricco di stimoli.

Facciamo tesoro delle differenze che caratterizzano noi, la nostra clientela e il mondo che ci circonda: dall’incontro delle nostre unicità nasce nuovo valore.

We are sustainable #SUSTAINABILITY

Agiamo ogni giorno per un futuro più sostenibile.

Contribuiamo in modo attivo e misurabile al raggiungimento degli obiettivi globali, generando un impatto concreto sul pianeta e sulla società.

We care #CUSTOMER

Conquistare ogni giorno la fiducia dei clienti è la nostra ambizione.

Puntiamo a offrire esperienze di valore e servizi innovativi, grazie a soluzioni di qualità che rispondano alle esigenze e costruiscano un futuro migliore.

DESCRIZIONE DEL RUOLO

In Fastweb+Vodafone stiamo cercando “AI Scientist” per il nostro Centro di Eccellenza AI (CoE AI). La risorsa sarà inserita in un team di lavoro e contribuirà in prima persona allo sviluppo delle soluzioni tecniche alla base del progetto.

Il CoE AI è il cuore dell'innovazione AI in Fastweb, dove sviluppiamo soluzioni di Machine Learning e Generative AI applicate a molteplici ambiti di business: dall'ottimizzazione di rete alla customer experience, dalla gestione documentale all'automazione dei processi.

In qualità di AI Scientist le tue attività e responsabilità saranno:


Sviluppare modelli di Machine Learning utilizzando i principali algoritmi e tecniche (supervised, unsupervised, classificazione, regressione, tecniche di validazione e ottimizzazione).
Utilizzare e integrare modelli di Generative AI in soluzioni business-oriented (chatbot, assistenti digitali, sistemi documentali intelligenti).
Implementare agenti AI (multi-step reasoning, tool calling, integrazione con API esterne).
Definire e monitorare metriche di qualità, performance e bias dei modelli.
Scrivere codice Python di qualità, sviluppare pipeline dati e modelli, eseguire esperimenti e analisi.
Operare sulla piattaforma cloud AWS per il training, deployment e monitoraggio dei modelli.
Collaborare a stretto contatto con il team di progetto, contribuendo alla roadmap e rispettando le milestone di progetto.
Interagire con gli stakeholder di progetto, recependo indicazioni e presentando i risultati del lavoro.
Supportare nella preparazione di documentazione tecnica e materiali di progetto.
Partecipare alla progettazione e implementazione di soluzioni end-to-end, dall'analisi dei dati al deployment in produzione.


REQUISITI ESSENZIALI


Laurea in discipline STEM (Informatica, Matematica, Statistica, Ingegneria o affini).
Conoscenza solida dei principali algoritmi e tecniche di Machine Learning (supervised, unsupervised, modelli di classificazione/regressione, tecniche di validazione e ottimizzazione).
Familiarità con i modelli generativi e capacità di integrarli in soluzioni applicative (LLM, prompt engineering, RAG, agentic AI).
Ottima conoscenza di Python e dei principali framework per ML/GenAI (scikit-learn, PyTorch, LangChain/Graph/Flow o simili).
Esperienza con cloud platform AWS (SageMaker, Bedrock, Lambda, S3) o disponibilità ad apprendere rapidamente.
Capacità di lavorare in team, orientamento al risultato e proattività nella risoluzione dei problemi.
Buona conoscenza della lingua inglese (B2+).
Soft skills: curiosità intellettuale, attenzione alla qualità del codice, disponibilità a imparare e a contribuire alla documentazione di progetto.


ALTRE INFORMAZIONI

Sede di lavoro: Milano + modalità ibrida

Contratto: Tempo Indeterminato

Orario: Full Time

CCNL di Riferimento: Telecomunicazioni

Retribuzione: da € 42.000 lordi/anno + variabile

La proposta economica finale potrà essere superiore al valore indicato e sarà definita valutando le competenze dimostrate, il livello di autonomia e l’effettivo perimetro di responsabilità assunto nel ruolo.

Siamo un luogo di lavoro inclusivo ed attento a garantire pari opportunità senza distinzione di genere, identità di genere, orientamento sessuale, etnia, età, religione e disabilità.

Title: Senior Data Scientist | Milan
Description:
Senior Data Scientist | Milan




Up to €95,000 + bonus

Hybrid Working in Central Milan




We’re hiring a Senior Data Scientist for a global organisation operating at the intersection of data, analytics, and AI-driven decision-making. This is a highly influential role, sitting at the forefront of data science strategy and shaping how advanced analytics and AI are applied across complex, real-world problems.

You’ll work closely with senior stakeholders and global engineering and product teams to deliver high-impact, data-driven solutions, including an AI-powered platform focused on risk and resilience.




As Senior Data Scientist, you will:

Define and execute a data science strategy aligned to product and business objectives
Lead the design, development, and deployment of models and algorithms into production
Analyse large, complex datasets to identify patterns and trends, translating insights into actionable product recommendations
Collaborate with global teams across product, engineering, and analytics to deliver high-quality, scalable solutions
Establish and uphold best practices across data science methodologies, including data preparation, model validation, and performance monitoring
Stay close to industry trends and emerging technologies, particularly in AI and GenAI




What we’re looking for

Strong, proven experience across Data Science, with exposure to Data Engineering and/or Machine Learning
Ability to operate autonomously within a global, distributed organisation
Solid experience selecting and applying appropriate methodologies, technologies, and techniques
Hands-on experience with data querying, manipulation, and analysis, including tools such as Pandas and NumPy




Nice to have

Experience working with Generative AI tools and approaches
Software engineering or programming experience, ideally in Python
Advanced academic background in Computer Science, Data Science, or equivalent industry experience




Up to €95,000 + bonus

Hybrid Working in Central Milan




If you’re a senior data scientist who enjoys combining strategic influence with hands-on technical depth, please apply or email wiil.semple@oliverbernard.com

Title: AI Engineer Junior
Description:
Stiamo supportando un nostro cliente nella ricerca di un/una AI Engineer Junior da inserire in un progetto innovativo legato all’applicazione dell’intelligenza artificiale al contesto manifatturiero.

La risorsa entrerà a far parte di un’iniziativa ad alto impatto, con l’obiettivo di sviluppare soluzioni AI a supporto dei processi produttivi, lavorando su dati industriali reali.




Cosa farà

Il/la candidato/a contribuirà allo sviluppo di sistemi per:

analisi delle performance produttive e individuazione di pattern e anomalie
supporto alla costruzione di modelli predittivi (es. efficienza, fermi macchina)
analisi dei dati di pianificazione e sviluppo di semplici simulazioni
implementazione di logiche di supporto alle decisioni operative

L’attività si baserà su dati provenienti da produzione, manutenzione, qualità, pianificazione ed ERP.




Requisiti

esperienza di 1–2 anni, anche tramite stage o apprendistato, in ambito AI, Data Science o Machine Learning
buona conoscenza di Python (es. pandas, basi di machine learning)
familiarità con dati strutturati e non strutturati
interesse per integrazione con sistemi (API, database)
approccio pratico e orientamento all’apprendimento

Plus

conoscenza di LLM o strumenti di intelligenza artificiale applicata
esperienza su progetti dati (anche accademici)
interesse per il settore industriale/manifatturiero




Informazioni:

possibilità di evoluzione verso un ruolo AI Engineer più senior
RAL indicativa: 28.000 – 34.000 €, commisurata all’esperienza
Sede provincia di Cremona

Title: Senior Data Scientist
Description:
Enabling What’s Next




Who We Are😎

Born in 2004 as Techedge, we have more than 20 years of expertise that enabled us to become what we are today: Avvale!

This path led us to be able to count on over 3500 employees, present in 12 countries around the world.




What We Do🌱

We help public and private companies to re-design their business models through the development of innovative and sustainable solutions, which have a positive impact not only on our customers but also on the world we live in!




How we do💡

Through circular economy models, made possible thanks to technological innovation.




What you will find 🔍

In Avvale we value our talents and your path with us will be full of:

Flexibility, thanks to our policy you can work remotely in Italy, up to 5 days a week, and in SEE and Switzerland up to 5 months a year;

Training & development as you can participate to training on the job, certifications, foreign languages classes and you will have a yearly budget to buy training courses;

Gender equality & inclusion, the obtaining of Pdr 125 is a proof of Avvale commitment to an inclusive and fair work environment;

Compensation & Benefits: In line with our commitment to transparency and people development, the gross annual salary (RAL) for this position ranges between €31,000 and €44,000, commensurate with experience and skills. The position is covered by the 'CCNL Commercio e Terziario' (National Collective Labor Agreement for Commerce and Service Sector), alongside a benefits package that includes meal vouchers, wellbeing initiatives (including Wellhub), and growth opportunities.




Role and responsibilities:




You will be part of an area that takes care of Artificial Intelligence.




As a Data Scientist, you will work for customers and internal research departments on complex and innovative projects about genAI, NLP, demand forecasting, predictive maintenance, computer vision, and lots more, in various fields and sectors.

You will focus on solving complex problems: translating requirements into actionable rules and

deliverables.




You will be responsible for:

Analyzing business needs and proposing the most effective technical approach, choosing between classical AI, GenAI, or hybrid solutions.
Designing the end-to-end AI solution, translating business requirements into technical specifications.
Designing and implementing solutions based on Machine Learning and advanced statistical methods, as well as GenAI architectures (RAG, agents, LLM orchestration, prompt engineering, fine-tuning when needed).
Assessing when to rely on an LLM/GenAI solution versus a classical ML approach, based on robustness, accuracy, and cost-effectiveness.
Validating developed models with a focus on explainability, generalization, robustness, and production behavior (including GenAI-specific risks such as hallucinations, bias, and inference costs).
Preparing and presenting technical and non-technical reports, adapting the storytelling to the audience's level.
Staying up to date on developments in Data Science, ML, and GenAI, proposing new ideas and participating in pre-sales activities.




Required Skills🛹

At least 2 years of relevant experience designing and implementing AI/ML solutions.
Hands-on experience applying advanced analytics/ML techniques to real business cases, with a proven ability to synthesize complex data and present results.
Practical experience with advanced analytics/ML techniques and GenAI solutions applied to real business cases .
Strong proficiency in Python and core AI frameworks/libraries.




Come with us💪

You will have the opportunity to work and get in touch with international players who will allow you to increase your know-how in view of future challenges!

(NEO)You will have the opportunity to define a “tailor made” growth path by choosing from countless courses made available for you that will allow you to deepen technical issues and increase your know-how!




We do not consider applications without CV.

Avvale promotes equal opportunity. We enhance diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment in compliance with applicable non-discrimination and data protection laws.

You will focus on solving complex problems: translating requirements into actionable rules and

deliverables.


You will be responsible for:

  • Analyzing business needs and proposing the most effective technical approach, choosing between classical AI, GenAI, or hybrid solutions.
  • Designing the end-to-end AI solution, translating business requirements into technical specifications.
  • Designing and implementing solutions based on Machine Learning and advanced statistical methods, as well as GenAI architectures (RAG, agents, LLM orchestration, prompt engineering, fine-tuning when needed).
  • Assessing when to rely on an LLM/GenAI solution versus a classical ML approach, based on robustness, accuracy, and cost-effectiveness.
  • Validating developed models with a focus on explainability, generalization, robustness, and production behavior (including GenAI-specific risks such as hallucinations, bias, and inference costs).
  • Preparing and presenting technical and non-technical reports, adapting the storytelling to the audience's level.
  • Staying up to date on developments in Data Science, ML, and GenAI, proposing new ideas and participating in pre-sales activities.


Required Skills🛹

  • At least 2 years of relevant experience designing and implementing AI/ML solutions.
  • Hands-on experience applying advanced analytics/ML techniques to real business cases, with a proven ability to synthesize complex data and present results.
  • Practical experience with advanced analytics/ML techniques and GenAI solutions applied to real business cases .
  • Strong proficiency in Python and core AI frameworks/libraries.


Come with us💪

You will have the opportunity to work and get in touch with international players who will allow you to increase your know-how in view of future challenges!

(NEO)You will have the opportunity to define a “tailor made” growth path by choosing from countless courses made available for you that will allow you to deepen technical issues and increase your know-how!


We do not consider applications without CV.

Avvale promotes equal opportunity. We enhance diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment in compliance with applicable non-discrimination and data protection laws.

Title: Data Science Manager
Description:
About us

Sky is the technology and media company that makes entertainment and connectivity a personal, simple, and seamless experience.

Our motto is We Believe in Better: we believe people deserve the very best, and we innovate every day to create value and simplify our customers’ lives.

As part of Sky Group and Comcast, we operate in an international environment driven by continuous innovation and powered by world-class technology that allows us to reach millions of people every day.

With an IP-first approach, we design cutting-edge technology platforms – Sky Stream, Sky Q, Sky Glass, and NOW – that bring together premium content, trusted news, and world-class sports within a simple and intuitive ecosystem.

We connect with audiences across Italy through our free-to-air channels – TV8, Cielo, and Sky TG24 – and go beyond entertainment with Sky Wifi and Sky Mobile, offering ultra-fast connectivity at home and on the go.

This is how we deliver the joy of a better experience, guided by our values:

Creative. We are ambitious and innovative, embracing new technologies and leveraging insights to stay one step ahead, continuously raising the bar for our customers.

Welcoming. We are inclusive, collaborative, and respectful, creating a strong sense of belonging.

Simplifying. We focus on what matters most, set clear priorities, bring clarity, and remove complexity.

Do the Right Thing. We build a culture of clarity, fairness, and trust, supported by transparent processes and decisions people can rely on.




Role: Manager Data Scientist

We are looking for a Data Science Manager who combines strong hands-on expertise with strategic leadership.

This is not a traditional people-management role: you will operate primarily as a senior individual contributor, while acting as the data science lead on high-impact, cross-functional initiatives. You will own complex projects end-to-end, partnering with business and technology stakeholders to turn data into measurable business impact.

You will play a pivotal role in bridging business challenges and advanced analytics, shaping how data science is applied across the organization and elevating both its impact and maturity.

What you will do:

Lead data science projects from problem framing to production deployment
Make key decisions on methodology, trade-offs, and technical direction
Develop and deploy machine learning and AI models to drive customer value and operational efficiency
Act as Data Lead on strategic initiatives, and serve as the go-to data science expert on critical, cross-functional programs
Translate ambiguous business questions into structured analytical problems
Drive alignment between stakeholders and ensure delivery of tangible, measurable outcomes
Stay deeply hands-on
Apply advanced analytics techniques in complex, multi-source and big data environments
Promote high standards in code quality, reproducibility, and model governance
Support adoption of responsible and ethical AI practices
Provide guidance and mentorship to junior data scientists
Foster a culture of ownership, pragmatism, and continuous improvement
Partner with business stakeholders to identify high-impact opportunities
Communicate complex analytical insights in a clear, compelling, and actionable way
Drive data adoption and data-driven decision making across the organization




Your profile:

Almeno 4 years of experience in data science or advanced analytics roles
Proven ability to own projects end-to-end in production environments
Strong track record of working in ambiguous, fast-paced environments
Natural ownership mindset: you don’t just build models—you drive impact
Machine learning (supervised, unsupervised, semi-supervised)
Statistical modeling and experimentation
Advanced proficiency in: Python (Pandas, NumPy, Scikit-learn, etc.), SQL, and/or R
Productionization and collaboration with engineering teams
Solid understanding of Data pipelines and large-scale data processing
Experience with version control (Git)
Ability to connect data work to real business outcomes and KPIs
Experience interacting with stakeholders and influencing decisions
Capability to identify and prioritize high-value use cases
Experience leading projects or initiatives, even without direct reports
Experience mentoring or coaching other data scientists
Fluency in English and Italian is required




What we offer:

For employees covered by the Private Radio and Television Collective Labour Agreement (CCNL), compensation starts from €51,000 gross per year.

Permanent employment contract.
Compensation commensurate with experience and specific skills.
Eligibility for the annual incentive scheme.
Access to Sky’s wellbeing programme, Sky4You, designed to support employees throughout different stages of life. Benefits include:
Free access to Sky TV services through Sky Q or Sky Stream.
Sky Wifi and Sky Mobile at exclusive employee rates.
Private healthcare insurance extendable to family members and cohabitants.
Life and accident insurance.
Free access to the company gym (Milan office only).
Financial support for new parents and caregivers.
Free tax return assistance.
Meal vouchers.
Additional initiatives focused on mental, physical, and financial wellbeing, summer camps and language courses for children, as well as a range of discounts and partnerships.




Hybrid Working

Hybrid working that truly works.

Depending on your role, you can benefit from up to 12 days per month of remote working, while our offices provide inspiring spaces where people collaborate, innovate, and make decisions together.

Our Milan hub is located in Rogoredo, Santa Giulia district, just a few minutes’ walk from Milano Rogoredo Station (M3 Rogoredo FS, regional and high-speed trains, suburban rail services), with convenient bus connections and easy access by car or motorcycle.

At the Sky Campus, you'll find cafés, a company restaurant, Sky Cinema, and collaborative spaces designed to foster innovation and idea-sharing. The surrounding area also offers restaurants, bars, supermarkets, and a wide range of services.




Learning & Development:

At Sky, we invest in your growth.

We believe in internal mobility as a key driver of professional development and offer opportunities to explore new roles and career paths across the organization.

Through Sky Learn, our learning platform, you will have access to continuous training, digital learning content, and upskilling and reskilling programmes to help you grow, evolve, and build your future career.




Social Impact & Inclusion

At Sky, we believe in people.

We are committed to creating a diverse, equitable, and inclusive workplace where everyone can be themselves and achieve their full potential. We actively support wellbeing and ensure equal opportunities for all, including candidates belonging to protected categories under Italian Law 68/99.

Sky hosts several employee networks, including Body&Mind, LGBTQ+, Women@Sky, and Parents&Carers—voluntary communities that bring colleagues together to promote wellbeing, inclusion, and empowerment initiatives.

We strive every day to create a lasting positive impact on our communities through Sky Cares volunteering programmes, Sky Up digital education initiatives, and immersive educational experiences offered through the Academy Studios.




Why Wait?

Apply now to build an extraordinary career and join a brilliant team.

We can’t wait to meet you. At Sky, ideas become experiences for millions of people.




Sky welcomes every form of talent. All applications, including those from candidates belonging to protected categories under Italian Law 68/99, will be considered based on equal opportunity and inclusion principles, without discrimination of any kind. Sky Italia guarantees a fair, inclusive, and non-discriminatory recruitment process.

In line with this commitment and Legislative Decree 96/2026, Sky Italia does not request or collect information regarding candidates’ previous compensation or employment grading, either during interviews or through any third parties involved in the recruitment process.
