Title: Machine Learning Engineer Job ID: JP054754
Description:
About the job Machine Learning Engineer Job ID: JP054754Machine Learning Engineer

We are looking for a Machine Learning Engineer to design, build, deploy, and maintain production-ready machine learning solutions that enhance business processes and digital services. You will work closely with data engineers, software developers, architects, and business stakeholders to deliver scalable, reliable, and compliant ML solutions across both Azure and on-premises environments.

Key Responsibilities


Prepare, transform, and validate data from multiple sources for machine learning use cases.
Design, train, test, and optimize ML models for classification, regression, forecasting, scoring, and detection.
Develop feature engineering processes and ensure data quality and reproducibility.
Deploy machine learning models into production through APIs, backend services, and batch processes.
Build and maintain ML pipelines, CI/CD workflows, and MLOps processes.
Monitor model performance, data drift, model drift, latency, and system reliability.
Implement version control for code, datasets, configurations, and ML models.
Collaborate with cross-functional teams to integrate ML solutions into existing applications.
Document technical implementations and contribute to ML engineering best practices.
Ensure solutions comply with relevant regulations, including the European AI Act.


Required Skills & Experience


Strong experience with Python for data science and machine learning development.
Experience with machine learning frameworks such as Scikit-learn, PyTorch, LangGraph, Ollama, and related open-source tools.
Knowledge of feature engineering, data preparation, model validation, and SQL.
Experience deploying ML components into production applications and backend services.
Familiarity with Docker, CI/CD pipelines, version control, and deployment of ML services.
Experience with MLOps, experiment tracking, model registry, workflow orchestration, and Azure DevOps.
Knowledge of monitoring tools for ML systems, including logging, metrics, drift detection, OpenTelemetry, and Dynatrace.
Experience with Azure and C#/.NET/Blazor is an advantage.


Languages


Native-level Dutch or French.
Good understanding of the second national language.


Work Arrangement


Hybrid role with 2 days onsite per week in Brussels and 3 days remote.

Title: BE - Data Scientist
Description:
Overview

Who are we?

CBTW is a global tech solutions company.

We empower businesses with tailored tech and business solutions that drive innovation and sustainable growth.

With over 2 500 experts across 21 countries, we deliver results through flexible engagement models like: whether augmenting teams, managing complex projects, or building long-term operations.

Thanks to our 5 service lines, we specialize in:


AI & Data Platforms,
Cloud & Enterprise Platforms,
Cybersecurity,
Software Engineering,
Strategy, Product & Transformation.


More info on our website: https://cbtw.tech/

What service line will you be part of?

As a Data Scientist, you will join our AI & Data Platforms expertise.

AI & Data Platforms is CBTW's service line dedicated to supporting organizations in leveraging their data and fulfilling their AI ambitions to achieve tangible results. We focus on adoption and measurable impact from day one, combining our comprehensive, end-to-end expertise with our ecosystem of leading partners such as SAS, Snowflake, Microsoft and Databricks.

Our clients rely on us to support with:


Data Platform modernisation, migration and optimisation
AI-driven Business Insights & Customer Analytics
Cutting-edge GenAI products & Agentic AI automation
Unified Data Platform & Cloud Infrastructure
Data & AI Governance
BI & Analytics for business teams


Responsibilities

What is your role?

You will be part of a journey where you will have two impacts: a client impact and an internal impact. For the internal impact, based on your skills and time, you will be joining our internal data community at CBTW, to share knowledge and best practices. For the client impact, tasks can vary depending on the client and the project, but as a Data Engineer, you will mainly:


Mining both structured and unstructured data to identify patterns and provide information through predictive models
Explore and collect data from multiple sources to apply or develop analytical ad statistical methods and machine learning models
Apply statistics and machine learning algorithms to discover patterns in datasets


Qualifications

What are we offering?

EVOLUTION:


A career development expert will follow up on your professional path within the company and support you in shaping and growing your career,
Catalogue with a wide variety of trainings for hard and soft skills, private language classes, unlimited access to an E-learning platform (Udemy, Code Academy... ) and possibility to get certified every year,
Training days credits.


COLLABORATION:


You will join a community of [job title] experts where colleagues support each other on projects, share knowledge through workshops and events to keep learning and evolving, and build international connections, while also enjoying great moments and drinks together,
Participate in internal projects to innovate and develop our solutions portfolio,
Join one of our collectives (Wellness, Agile, RSE, Gaming ...) and share your interests with other positive thinkers,
You’ll also enjoy several unforgettable company parties throughout the year. Events that CBTW has truly mastered the art of organizing.


ON TOP OF THAT:


We offer a competitive salary with a flexible mobility package that allows you to choose options such as having mobility benefits (electric cars, electric bikes, public transportation subscription).


Your next adventure starts at CBTW!

Do you see a match? We would love to see your application coming in! 😉

Title: Expert in AI/NLP/ML
HTML:
We currently have a vacancy for an Expert in AI/NLP/ML fluent in English, to offer his/her services as an expert who will be based in Brussels, Belgium. The work will be carried out either in the company's premises or on-site at the customer's premises. In the context of the first assignment, the successful candidate will be integrated into the Development team of the company that will closely cooperate with a major client's IT team on-site.

Tasks:

    • Engaging with Business stakeholders, organising workshops and meetings to gather requirements;
    • Analysing needs in the archiving domain and translating them into robust technical specifications;
    • Designing and proposing AI- and cloud-based architectures that are secure, scalable, and costeffective;
    • Developing, testing, and deploying solutions leveraging Large Language Models (LLMs), vector databases, and orchestration frameworks (e.g., LangChain);
    • Building prototypes, Proofs of Concept, and production-ready services using Python and Jupyter Notebooks;
    • Leveraging AWS Cloud Services (e.g., S3, Lambda, SageMaker, Bedrock, API Gateway, ECS/EKS, CloudFormation/Terraform);
    • Preparing and maintaining technical documentation, ensuring knowledge transfer within the team;
    • Integrating solutions into existing data and archiving systems;
    • Monitoring, optimising, and supporting deployed solutions over time;
    • Collaborating closely with Business, IT Infrastructure, and Security teams to ensure long-term sustainability.



Requirements

    • Master's degree in IT or a relevant discipline, combined with a minimum of 13 years of relevant working experience in IT;
    • At least 3 years of experience in LLMs: fine-tuning, prompting strategies, evaluation, and deployment;
    • At least 2 years of experience in LangChain or equivalent frameworks for LLM orchestration;
    • At least 2 years of experience in Vector databases;
    • At least 2 years of experience in Python, with practical use of Jupyter Notebooks;
    • At least 2 years of experience in AWS services for AI, data, and cloud-native development;
    • At least 1 year of experience in Machine Learning Operations, including CI/CD pipelines and monitoring of AI solutions;
    • At least 1 year of experience in data engineering and archiving best practices;
    • Good knowledge of Docker, Kubernetes and security best practices in AI and cloud-based deployments would be an advantage;
    • Knowledge of DevOps/Infrastructure-as-Code tools beyond AWS (Terraform, Ansible) and data governance frameworks and compliance standards (e.g., GDPR) would be an advantage;
    • AWS Solutions Architect, AWS Machine Learning, AWS DevOps Engineer, TensorFlow Developer or equivalent AI/ML credentials, Kubernetes Administrator (CKA), TOGAF or equivalent enterprise architecture framework certifications would be an asset;
    • Excellent command of the English language.


Benefits

If you are seeking a career in an exciting, dynamic and multicultural international environment with exciting opportunities that will boost your career, please send us your detailed CV in English, quoting reference (19007/09/2025).

We offer a competitive remuneration (either on contract basis or remuneration with full benefits package), based on qualifications and experience. All applications will be treated as confidential.

You may also consider all our other open vacancies by visiting the career section of our web site (www.eurodyn.com) and follow us on Twitter (@EURODYN_Careers) and LinkedIn.

EUROPEAN DYNAMICS (www.eurodyn.com) is a leading European Software, Information and Communication Technologies company, operating internationally (Athens, Brussels, Luxembourg, Copenhagen, Berlin, Stockholm, London, Nicosia, Valetta, Vienna, Den Haag, Hong Kong, etc.) The company employs over 1000 engineers, IT experts and consultants (around 3% PhD, 36% MSc and 53% BSc). We design and develop software applications using integrated, state-of-the-art technology. Our current IT projects have a value exceeding 300 million EURO. EUROPEAN DYNAMICS is a renowned supplier of IT services to European Union Institutions, international organizations, European Agencies and national government Administrations in 40 countries and 4 continents.

As part of our dedication to the diversity of our workforce, we are committed to Equal Employment Opportunity without regard for race, colour, age, national origin, ethnicity, gender, disability, sexual orientation, gender identity or religion.

EUROPEAN DYNAMICS (ED) adheres to the General Data Protection Regulation principles by applying its Privacy Policy as published in www.eurodyn.com/privacy. By submitting an application to this position and by sharing your personal data with ED, you acknowledge and accept its Policy and authorise ED to process your personal data for the purposes of the company's recruitment opportunities, in line to the Policy.

Title: Data Scientist
Description:
Company Description

Sopra Steria offers tailored, end-to-end corporate technology and software solutions to help clients make bold choices and deliver results. Successfully so! With more than 56.000 colleagues in 30 countries, we rank as Europe’s leading digital solutions provider. Some of the most successful companies in Europe rely on our technology due to our commitment to innovation, collaboration, and value in business development.

The world is how we shape it. Let’s shape it together.

Job Description

Are you brimming with fresh ideas to lead our Data Science team in providing data-driven solutions to our customers based on the latest machine learning models and deep learning algorithms? If you are looking for a new challenge with a steep learning curve, and the support of a dynamic team, you may be our next Data Scientist.

As a Data Scientist, you will dive into the world of artificial intelligence. You will apply your skills to real scenarios and in different domains, such as drug discovery in healthcare, fraud detection in banks, the development of recommendation systems in the retail sector, and more.

Qualifications

We’re seeking passionate colleagues who are eager to push the boundaries in digital transformation and technology consulting. At Sopra Steria, you’ll have the opportunity to grow your skills in a constructive, collaborative team environment, working on impactful projects that drive change for our clients. If you thrive on challenge and meet (most of) the qualifications below, we look forward to your application!

You are fully convinced that data science isn’t a hype, but you also believe that, in order to be valuable and really make a company smarter, it must be done right. Your goal is therefore to create the most impactful solution possible in the quickest and easiest way possible so that real value can be extracted from data and artificial intelligence.

You’ll have knowledge and experience of the following:


At least 3+ years of experience in Data Science.
Master's degree (or equivalent through experience) in a field that is highly data-driven.
Experience with some of the following data science techniques: supervised and unsupervised learning models, deep learning, anomaly detection, recommender systems, natural language processing, computer vision and reinforcement learning.
Well versed in open-source software and libraries in Python and/or R for machine and deep learning.
Extensive experience in building and implementing advanced analytics solutions backed up with some reference cases.
Experience with big data and platforms such as Azure Machine Learning Services and Amazon SageMaker is a plus.
Experience in one of the following languages: Python, R, Spark, Scala, Java or C++.
You are resident in Belgium and look forward to working a few days a week at the client's office.
You're fluent in Dutch or French, have a strong grasp of the other, and speak English fluently as well.


What We Can Offer You

As a member of one of Europe’s largest digital solutions providers, you’ll benefit from extensive career development opportunities, both local and international. At the Sopra Steria Academy, you’ll be part of a dynamic network of 56,000 professionals at all stages of their careers. With a wide array of offices to explore, you can find your ideal location and take the next step in your career. 

You’ll become a part of a major Tech player in Europe recognized for its consulting, digital services, and software development. On top of that, you’ll join the OptimAi Team: the partner for advanced, intelligent solutions using AI.

Additional Information

Our employees are known for making bold choices and delivering results. Work among high-level professionals at the forefront of corporate software solutions and innovation at Europe’s Leading Digital Service Provider.  

We offer a generous employee benefits package that includes:


A company car or mobility budget
Laptop, phone & phone subscription
A competitive salary and indefinite contract
Insurance coverage, meal vouchers, eco-cheques, and more
Continuous learning opportunities through the Sopra Steria Academy to support your career development


People are the cornerstone of our success. That’s why we aspire to be bolder together. Our goal is to build high-functioning teams and healthy team environments that inspire and help each other to deliver excellence for each of our customers.  

Excited about this job opportunity? Ready to shape the world with us? Great! We are looking forward to your application! 

______

Sopra Steria is an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will be considered for employment without regard to age, ancestry, nationality, color, family or medical leave, gender identity or expression, genetic information, immigration status, marital status, medical condition, national origin, physical or mental disability, political affiliation, protected veteran or military status, race, ethnicity, religion, gender (including pregnancy), sexual orientation or any other characteristic protected by applicable local laws, regulations and ordinances. We foster a work environment that is inclusive and respectful of all differences.

Title: Data Scientist - AI/ML (Remote)
Description:
Role: Data Scientist - AI/ML (Remote)
Location: Remote (Work from Anywhere)
Job Type: Contract
Payout: $30 - $100/hour




Role Overview:

We are hiring for one of our clients, seeking a Data Scientist (ML) to work on a contract basis. The role involves developing and deploying machine learning models to improve predictive accuracy for client datasets. You will collaborate with cross-functional teams to identify data-driven solutions and automate workflows.




Key Responsibilities:

• Design, train, and optimize machine learning models for classification, regression, and clustering tasks.

• Preprocess and analyze large-scale structured and unstructured datasets to extract actionable insights.

• Implement and maintain MLOps pipelines for model deployment, monitoring, and retraining.

• Develop and document reproducible experiments using Python, Jupyter notebooks, and version control systems.

• Present findings and recommendations to stakeholders through clear visualizations and technical reports.




Required Skills & Qualifications:

• Proficiency in Python and relevant libraries such as scikit-learn, TensorFlow, or PyTorch.

• Experience with SQL for data extraction, transformation, and analysis.

• Strong background in statistical modeling and experimental design.

• Familiarity with cloud platforms including AWS, GCP, or Azure for model deployment.

• Ability to work independently in a remote environment with minimal supervision.




More About the Opportunity:

This role offers a unique opportunity to work with a global leader in the Software Development industry, contributing to the scalability of AI-driven solutions. You will gain exposure to cutting-edge tools and collaborate with experts in data science and engineering.




Equal Opportunity Employer:

We hire based on skills and expertise. All qualified candidates are welcome regardless of background, experience, or prior employment history. Applications are reviewed solely on demonstrated technical ability and qualifications.

Title: Data Scientist
Description:

In this role, you'll work on a diverse portfolio of projects, applying advanced analytics and AI to solve complex operational challenges. Areas of focus include predictive analytics, security insights, energy optimization, generative AI solutions, and the development of strategic data models that drive smarter decision-making.

Key Responsibilities


Design, develop, and deploy advanced data science solutions using Machine Learning, Deep Learning, and Generative AI techniques.
Translate complex business challenges into scalable, data-driven solutions.
Collaborate with cross-functional stakeholders, including business, operations, IT, and other technical teams.
Apply best practices throughout the model lifecycle, from development and validation to deployment and monitoring.
Promote the adoption of modern data science methodologies, tools, and industry best practices.



Your Profile


Bachelor's or Master's degree in Data Science, Computer Science, Statistics, Artificial Intelligence, or a related field.
At least 5 years of experience in Data Science, with a proven track record of delivering industrial or enterprise-scale analytics solutions.
Hands-on experience with cloud-based platforms for developing and deploying production-ready data analytics and AI solutions.
Strong programming skills and solid knowledge of a broad range of data science, machine learning, and AI techniques.
Demonstrated experience bringing ML and AI models into production environments.
Excellent analytical and problem-solving skills, with the ability to convert complex business requirements into actionable insights.
Strong communication and presentation skills.
Fluent in English.
Available to work from the client's office two days per week (Tuesday and Thursday).

Title: AI Specialist (Junior - Medior)
Description:
Company Description

Are you ready to shape the future technological landscape in Europe?

We are dedicated to responsible digitalisation, building innovative, inclusive solutions that drive meaningful impact. With over 8,900 professionals across Europe, we help EU institutions, public and private organisations thrive in a rapidly evolving digital world. Are you ready to shape the future through technology? Your journey starts here. Take a look at some of our impactful projects here: https://netcompany.com/cases/

Job Description

Are you ready to join the forefront of technology as a AI Specialist in Netcompany?

As An AI Specialist You Will


Analyse, develop, test, and deploy AI-driven solutions, proof of concepts, pilot projects, and production-ready applications.
Contribute to the evolution and production rollout of enterprise AI platforms, chatbots, and related AI services.
Support the automation and optimisation of document management, legal document tagging, and knowledge retrieval processes using AI technologies.
Develop and enhance Generative AI solutions leveraging Large Language Models (LLMs), embeddings, vector databases, and AI APIs.
Collaborate with business and technical stakeholders to translate functional requirements into scalable AI solutions and provide technical guidance.
Prepare technical documentation, architecture deliverables, and reusable building blocks contributing to AI platforms and reference architectures.
Participate in AI solution deployment, operationalisation, scaling, monitoring, and production support activities.
Contribute to AI standards, best practices, training initiatives, and continuous improvement within the AI ecosystem.
Work closely with multidisciplinary teams in an agile and fast-evolving technology environment.


Qualifications

What would make you a fit for the role:


University degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, Artificial Intelligence, Data Science, or a related field.
Experience with programming languages used in data analytics and Artificial Intelligence, particularly Python and Java.
Knowledge of prompt engineering techniques, including prompt design, evaluation, optimisation, and analysis of model behaviour.
Hands-on experience with Generative AI technologies, Large Language Models, embeddings, vector databases, open-source LLMs, and AI APIs.
Understanding of AI solution architecture, containerisation, private cloud environments, CI/CD practices, and MLOps methodologies.
Experience with AI frameworks and technologies such as FastAPI, Spring Boot, Flask, vector databases (e.g. Qdrant), and document processing solutions is considered an asset.
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with the ability to troubleshoot complex technical challenges and assess evolving technology trends.
Excellent communication skills with the ability to explain technical concepts to both technical and non-technical stakeholders in English; knowledge of French is considered an asset.
Additional experience with SQL, ETL/ELT pipelines, data modelling, orchestration tools, data visualisation platforms, business case analysis, and maturity assessments would be considered an asset.


Additional Information

Being a part of the Netcompany team, you will be provided with:


Opportunity to work in a modern environment.
Competitive compensation & benefits package
Hospitalization plan
Hybrid working model
Meal allowance
Mobility budget or commuting allowance (if applicable)
Well-being activities (on premises)
Continuous learning opportunities using the most modern methods (unlimited access to Udemy for Business, ad-hoc trainings)
Personalized development plan for targeted career growth


If you are looking forward to be part of a diverse environment, and have the opportunity to work alongside well-experienced professionals, on challenging, large-scale projects that directly impact millions of citizens around the globe, then this is the place to be!

By joining Netcompany Belgium, you will be an integral part of a distinguished team of 600+ tech enthusiasts working for international projects, primarily within the European Institutions market, with a unique opportunity to shape the future of digital services in an environment that values ideas and fosters growth.

Please upload your CV in English via the Apply button. All applications will be treated as strictly confidential.

We ensure equal opportunities, treatment, and consideration to all candidates. Discrimination based on sex, racial or ethnic origin, religion or belief, disability, age, sexual orientation or marital status, physical or mental disability, or any other factor protected by applicable laws and regulations is prohibited. As part of the Netcompany culture, we respect human rights and focus on creating a positive workplace, where all employees are valued, and where diversity and inclusion are a vital part of our everyday working experience.

In the following link you may find our CV Submission privacy notice: https://netcompany.com/cv-submission-privacy-notice/

Title: Machine Learning Engineer (BE)
Description:
Job Description

At CluePoints, we’re redefining how clinical trials are run. As the premier provider of Risk-Based Quality Management (RBQM) and Data Quality Oversight software, we harness advanced statistics, artificial intelligence, and machine learning to ensure the quality, accuracy, and integrity of clinical trial data, helping life sciences organizations bring safer, more effective treatments to patients faster.

We’re proud to be an ambitious, fast-growing technology scale-up with a dynamic and diverse international team representing more than 40 nationalities. Collaboration, flexibility, and continuous learning are part of our DNA.

At CluePoints, you’ll find a culture where you can grow, make an impact, and have fun along the way. Guided by our values of Care, Passion, and Smart Disruption, we’re united by a shared mission: to create smarter ways to run efficient clinical trials and deliver AI-powered insights that improve human outcomes worldwide.

We are looking for an AI Engineer to join our AI Innovation team, focused on exploring, prototyping, and operationalizing cutting-edge AI solutions for clinical data review. This role sits at the intersection of research and engineering: you will investigate emerging AI capabilities—particularly LLMs and agentic AI systems—and translate them into impactful use cases within clinical trials. While the primary focus is on innovation and experimentation, you are expected to drive selected prototypes through to robust, production-ready solutions.

Job Requirements

Main Qualifications


Master’s or PhD in Computer Science, AI, Machine Learning, or a related quantitative field
Strong programming skills in Python
Proven experience working with LLMs and modern AI frameworks (e.g., OpenAI, Anthropic, Mistral, Meta)
Solid understanding of agentic AI concepts and orchestration frameworks (e.g.,
LangGraph, AutoGen, CrewAI, function calling)
Experience with Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) and/or hybrid AI systems
Strong software engineering fundamentals (modular design, APIs, version control with Git)


Preferred Qualifications (Nice to have)


Experience with clinical trial data (protocols, EDC, CTMS, AE/SAE reporting)
Familiarity with regulated environments (e.g., life sciences, healthcare)
Experience deploying AI/ML systems into production environments
Knowledge of vector databases, embeddings, and information retrieval systems
Background in NLP, deep learning, or applied AI research
Experience with data quality, statistical methods, or risk-based monitoring
Familiarity with GPU computing and performance optimization


Job Responsibilities

AI Innovation & Exploration


Explore and evaluate emerging AI techniques, with a focus on LLMs and agentic AI systems
Design and prototype novel AI-driven approaches for clinical data review (e.g., protocol interpretation, query generation, deviation detection, medical coding)
Translate scientific literature and industry trends into practical experimentation and prototypes
Identify new opportunities where AI can bring value within clinical research workflows


Agent & System Development


Architect and build intelligent agent systems leveraging LLMs, RAG, and tool augmentation
Integrate diverse data sources (e.g., protocols, clinical datasets, operational metadata) into AI workflows
Develop modular services, APIs, and reusable components to support AI applications
Prototype interactive interfaces (e.g., chat-based agents, copilots, APIs)


From Prototype to Production


Contribute to the industrialization of selected AI solutions, ensuring scalability and robustness
Apply best practices for evaluation, monitoring, and reproducibility of AI systems
Ensure outputs are traceable, auditable, and aligned with regulatory expectations
Collaborate with engineering teams to transition prototypes into production-ready systems


Collaboration & Thought Leadership


Work closely with clinical experts, product managers, and R&D teams to refine use cases
Communicate findings, trade-offs, and recommendations clearly to stakeholders
Stay at the forefront of AI advancements and contribute to CluePoints’ innovation strategy
Optionally contribute to publications, conferences, or internal knowledge sharing


Job Benefits

🇧🇪 What We Offer – Belgium


Health Insurance through Alan (100% hospitalisation cover, 80% ambulatory and dental)
Mobility Budget for eco-transport, housing, or car allowance (flexible 3-pillar system)
Group Insurance Plan with 6–12% employer pension contribution based on seniority
Meal Vouchers (€8/day) and Eco Vouchers for sustainable purchases
A hub-based hybrid model that blends flexibility with purpose — connecting teams through collaboration, learning, and a vibrant social culture.


Equal Opportunities & GDPR Notice

CluePoints is an equal opportunities employer. We value and respect diversity in our workforce and do not tolerate discrimination based on gender, age, disability, ethnic origin, religion, sexual orientation, or any other protected ground under Belgian law.

Personal data collected as part of your application will be processed in compliance with the EU GDPR and Belgian data protection legislation.

You have the right to access, correct, or delete your personal data at any time by contacting privacy@cluepoints.com.

Title: Data Scientist
Description:
Company Description

Sopra Steria offers tailored, end-to-end corporate technology and software solutions to help clients make bold choices and deliver results. Successfully so! With more than 56.000 colleagues in 30 countries, we rank as Europe’s leading digital solutions provider. Some of the most successful companies in Europe rely on our technology due to our commitment to innovation, collaboration, and value in business development.

The world is how we shape it. Let’s shape it together.

Job Description

Are you brimming with fresh ideas to lead our Data Science team in providing data-driven solutions to our customers based on the latest machine learning models and deep learning algorithms? If you are looking for a new challenge with a steep learning curve, and the support of a dynamic team, you may be our next Data Scientist.

As a Data Scientist, you will dive into the world of artificial intelligence. You will apply your skills to real scenarios and in different domains, such as drug discovery in healthcare, fraud detection in banks, the development of recommendation systems in the retail sector, and more.

Qualifications

We’re seeking passionate colleagues who are eager to push the boundaries in digital transformation and technology consulting. At Sopra Steria, you’ll have the opportunity to grow your skills in a constructive, collaborative team environment, working on impactful projects that drive change for our clients. If you thrive on challenge and meet (most of) the qualifications below, we look forward to your application!

You are fully convinced that data science isn’t a hype, but you also believe that, in order to be valuable and really make a company smarter, it must be done right. Your goal is therefore to create the most impactful solution possible in the quickest and easiest way possible so that real value can be extracted from data and artificial intelligence.

You’ll have knowledge and experience of the following:


At least 3+ years of experience in Data Science.
Master's degree (or equivalent through experience) in a field that is highly data-driven.
Experience with some of the following data science techniques: supervised and unsupervised learning models, deep learning, anomaly detection, recommender systems, natural language processing, computer vision and reinforcement learning.
Well versed in open-source software and libraries in Python and/or R for machine and deep learning.
Extensive experience in building and implementing advanced analytics solutions backed up with some reference cases.
Experience with big data and platforms such as Azure Machine Learning Services and Amazon SageMaker is a plus.
Experience in one of the following languages: Python, R, Spark, Scala, Java or C++.
You are resident in Belgium and look forward to working a few days a week at the client's office.
You're fluent in Dutch or French, have a strong grasp of the other, and speak English fluently as well.


What We Can Offer You

As a member of one of Europe’s largest digital solutions providers, you’ll benefit from extensive career development opportunities, both local and international. At the Sopra Steria Academy, you’ll be part of a dynamic network of 56,000 professionals at all stages of their careers. With a wide array of offices to explore, you can find your ideal location and take the next step in your career. 

You’ll become a part of a major Tech player in Europe recognized for its consulting, digital services, and software development. On top of that, you’ll join the OptimAi Team: the partner for advanced, intelligent solutions using AI.

Additional Information

Our employees are known for making bold choices and delivering results. Work among high-level professionals at the forefront of corporate software solutions and innovation at Europe’s Leading Digital Service Provider.  

We offer a generous employee benefits package that includes:


A company car or mobility budget
Laptop, phone & phone subscription
A competitive salary and indefinite contract
Insurance coverage, meal vouchers, eco-cheques, and more
Continuous learning opportunities through the Sopra Steria Academy to support your career development


People are the cornerstone of our success. That’s why we aspire to be bolder together. Our goal is to build high-functioning teams and healthy team environments that inspire and help each other to deliver excellence for each of our customers.  

Excited about this job opportunity? Ready to shape the world with us? Great! We are looking forward to your application! 

______

Sopra Steria is an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will be considered for employment without regard to age, ancestry, nationality, color, family or medical leave, gender identity or expression, genetic information, immigration status, marital status, medical condition, national origin, physical or mental disability, political affiliation, protected veteran or military status, race, ethnicity, religion, gender (including pregnancy), sexual orientation or any other characteristic protected by applicable local laws, regulations and ordinances. We foster a work environment that is inclusive and respectful of all differences.

Title: Data Scientist
Description:
We're looking for an experienced Data Scientist to join our client's team and contribute to innovative, data-driven initiatives that deliver real business impact. In this role, you'll work on a diverse portfolio of projects, applying advanced analytics and AI to solve complex operational challenges. Areas of focus include predictive analytics, security insights, energy optimization, generative AI solutions, and the development of strategic data models that drive smarter decision-making.

Key Responsibilities


Design, develop, and deploy advanced data science solutions using Machine Learning, Deep Learning, and Generative AI techniques.
Translate complex business challenges into scalable, data-driven solutions.
Collaborate with cross-functional stakeholders, including business, operations, IT, and other technical teams.
Apply best practices throughout the model lifecycle, from development and validation to deployment and monitoring.
Promote the adoption of modern data science methodologies, tools, and industry best practices.



Your Profile


Bachelor's or Master's degree in Data Science, Computer Science, Statistics, Artificial Intelligence, or a related field.
At least 5 years of experience in Data Science, with a proven track record of delivering industrial or enterprise-scale analytics solutions.
Hands-on experience with cloud-based platforms for developing and deploying production-ready data analytics and AI solutions.
Strong programming skills and solid knowledge of a broad range of data science, machine learning, and AI techniques.
Demonstrated experience bringing ML and AI models into production environments.
Excellent analytical and problem-solving skills, with the ability to convert complex business requirements into actionable insights.
Strong communication and presentation skills.
Fluent in English.
Available to work from the client's office two days per week (Tuesday and Thursday).



Contactperson & Reference

Title: Data Scientist - Banking
Description:
Data Scientist – Financial Services

About NORRIQ

NORRIQ is an international organization headquartered in Denmark and Belgium, with over 350 consultants dedicated to driving business transformation through innovative technology.

Our Financial Services (FS) practice includes 100+ specialists helping banks and financial institutions adapt to evolving client expectations, leverage cutting-edge technologies, and navigate increasingly complex regulatory environments.

At NORRIQ, we believe that people are our greatest asset. We foster a culture of innovation, growth, and excellence, offering tailored training, clear career paths, and a strong sense of community. Join us on this exciting journey and become a valued member of the NORRIQ family — connected for life!

The Opportunity

NORRIQ Financial Services is looking for a Data Scientist to accelerate our data analytics, machine learning, and generative AI initiatives. You will work with leading financial institutions to design, build, and deliver data-driven and AI-powered solutions that create tangible business impact.

Roles & Responsibilities

Client-Facing Responsibilities


Design and implement machine learning and generative AI solutions for complex financial use cases.
Conduct exploratory data analysis and build insightful dashboards or visualizations.
Present analytical findings and actionable recommendations to senior management and stakeholders.
Collaborate with cross-functional teams (Data Engineers, Business Analysts, Developers) to industrialize AI solutions.
Provide technical guidance and advisory on data science and AI best practices.


Technical Responsibilities


Develop and maintain AI and ML models using Python, scikit-learn, and deep learning frameworks.
Apply a wide range of analytical methodologies, including:
Generative AI techniques (Prompt Engineering, RAG, Fine-tuning, Speech-to-Text, Agentic Applications, Image Generation & Interpretation)
Predictive AI and classical machine learning approaches.
Contribute to production-grade AI applications using tools such as GitLab, DVC, and MLOps frameworks.
Ensure reproducibility, version control, and performance optimization across models and pipelines.
Create and maintain Jupyter notebooks for experimentation and documentation.

Internal Contributions


Contribute to NORRIQ’s AI & Data Science Center of Excellence through knowledge sharing and internal projects.
Support proposal writing and solution architecture for client engagements.
Stay at the forefront of developments in data science, AI, and GenAI frameworks (LangChain, Haystack, etc.).


Our Data Scientist Must Have


Fluency in English, and Dutch or French, with at least passive knowledge of the other
Belgian residency
At least few years of experience in a similar role.


What NORRIQ Offers You


Be part of a fast-growing, international organization with a unique and people-centered culture
Enjoy a highly competitive compensation package, including a company car, iPhone, and additional allowances
Access extensive training programs in Belgium and Denmark, focusing on both technical expertise and soft skills
Work with a dynamic and diverse team of experienced professionals and young talent in a collaborative environment within the financial services sector


Join NORRIQ to elevate your career, contribute to transformative projects, and make a lasting impact in the financial services industry!

Title: Consultant in Data Science
Description:
Sia is a next-generation, global management consulting group. Founded in 1999, we were born digital. Today our strategy and management capabilities are augmented by data science, enhanced by creativity and driven by responsibility. We’re optimists for change and we help clients initiate, navigate and benefit from transformation. We believe optimism is a force multiplier, helping clients to mitigate downside and maximize opportunity. With expertise across a broad range of sectors and services, our 3,000 consultants serve clients worldwide from 48 locations in 19 countries. Our expertise delivers results. Our optimism transforms outcomes.

Job Description

As a consultant in Data Science, you will be involved in transformation projects at our clients. You will execute project assignments in modelling and digital transformation (business/IT analysis and partial coding) environments with a good deal of business perspectives:


Machine learning, AI and statistics: developing predictive models of decision making (supervised and unsupervised learning algorithms, econometrics, forecasting, quantitative diagnosis,...)
Operational research : assistance in the design and implementation of constraint optimization solutions and simulation models
Big Data : Algorithmic knowledge for large and unstructured data processing


You will get the opportunity to actively contribute to Sia's internal activities:


Development and reinforcement of our offer, by understanding and interpreting data science innovation and challenges
Business development, by contributing to need definition and participating in our commercial initiatives


Equipped with the entrepreneurial fibre, you are ready to cooperate in a fast growing company.

In return, you can expect:


An international company that gives importance to diversity, inclusion, employee well-being and corporate social responsibilities actions.
A great working atmosphere with friendly and professional colleagues where innovation and entrepreneurship are encouraged
A personal development plan with a regular follow-up as well training opportunities to develop yourself
A competitive salary with great advantages, like mobility budget, hospital insurance, performance -related bonus,...


Qualifications


University Master Civil Engineer (applied mathematics, IT or similar)
0 to 3 years’ experience as a consultant
Outstanding team player with great interpersonal and communication skills
Strong interest in data science and related sectors such as Energy and Finance
Analytical mind, exploring options and able to synthesize into conclusions
You have a strong interest in data and AI-related topics, the ability to quickly build expertise in these areas, and you’re excited about helping our clients seize the opportunities they offer.
Dynamic, result oriented, rigorous and committed
Trilingual: French, Dutch and English
Prepared to participate in temporary assignments abroad


Additional Information

We are unable to provide a work permit for this position

Sia is an equal opportunity employer. All aspects of employment, including hiring, promotion, remuneration, or discipline, are based solely on performance, competence, conduct, or business needs.

Title: Data Scientist (near-site, Brussels) - MAIA
Description:
Position: Data engineer.

Location: Near-site (Brussels).

Rate: 570€/day.

Start date: To be confirmed (ASAP).

Language: Services will be provided in English. Consultants must have at least a B2 level, in accordance with the Common European Framework of Reference for Languages (CEFR).

Minimum post-secundary education required: at least 3 years of studies attested by a diploma.

Minimum professional experience required: a minimum of 5 years of professional experience, 3 of them in a similar position.

Responsibilities:


Design, develop, and maintain data integration processes that consolidate information from multiple sources for use by business systems and services.
Ensure the quality, accuracy, consistency, and accessibility of data across the organisation.
Implement and enforce data governance policies, including data access, integrity, security, and privacy controls.
Maintain and manage metadata repositories to support effective data governance and data lifecycle management.
Design, administer, and optimise relational and non-relational databases to ensure performance, scalability, and reliability.
Develop, optimise, and maintain SQL queries, stored procedures, and other database objects.
Manage database change processes, including deployment, version control, and documentation.
Define and administer database access rights and security permissions in accordance with organisational policies.
Support data architecture initiatives and contribute to the continuous improvement of data management practices and standards.


Specific expertise:


Proven experience with relational and non-relational database technologies, including Oracle, MySQL, PostgreSQL, and NoSQL databases.
Strong knowledge of database design, SQL development, performance tuning, and database administration.
Experience with programming and scripting languages for data processing and automation, such as Python and R.
Solid understanding of data engineering principles, including ETL/ELT processes, data modelling, and data pipeline development.
Experience with data governance and data management platforms such as Talend, Collibra, Microsoft Purview, Databricks, and Oracle data management solutions.
Proficiency in data visualisation and reporting tools, including Power BI, Qlik, and Tableau.
Experience working with cloud-based data platforms and services, such as Azure Synapse Analytics and AWS DataZone.
Good understanding of data governance, metadata management, data quality, and information security best practices.

Title: Data Scientist
Description:
An exciting opportunity for a skilled Data Scientist to join a dynamic team focused on advancing the field of biopharmaceutical manufacturing. This role will involve collaborating with various functions within the organization to drive innovation and excellence in data science applications related to drug substance manufacturing processes. You will play a crucial role in supporting the development and optimization of key processes and methodologies.

Responsibilities


Provide advanced statistical and data science support across multiple functions, including cell culture, purification, and analytical activities.
Engage in process optimization, investigation, comparability studies, monitoring, method transfer, validation, and characterization.
Contribute to the preparation of protocols and reports with significant statistical content through data collection, experiment design, and data analysis.
Ensure the traceability and correctness of collected data, facilitating timely information exchange with relevant stakeholders.
Maintain compliance with legal standards and regulations, ensuring high-quality scientific and technical documentation.



Technical Skills


Proficient in statistical data analysis and data science techniques.
Experience working in a cGMP environment, with a strong understanding of regulatory guidelines.
Domain knowledge in biologics, particularly in upstream or downstream development and manufacturing.
Ability to lead complex technical projects effectively.
Strong problem-solving skills with a change agent mentality to propose innovative solutions to scientific challenges.



Profile


Holds a Bachelor's degree in data sciences, statistics, or a related discipline with a minimum of 6 years of experience in the pharmaceutical industry; or a Master's degree with at least 4 years of experience; or a Ph.D. with a minimum of 2 years of experience.
Demonstrates effective communication skills, both written and verbal, for cross-functional collaboration, particularly in English. Proficiency in Dutch is advantageous.
A team player capable of working autonomously in a collaborative, dynamic environment.



Practical Information


This position is based at a biopharmaceutical manufacturing site near Geel.
Fluent English is required, and proficiency in Dutch is a plus.
This is a long-term opportunity available for both consultants on our payroll and freelance professionals.



Contactperson & Reference


Reference #: INCA26907
Casper Moyaert
casper.moyaert@i4m.be

Title: Experienced Recruiter of Externals Consultant
Description:
Mission Overview

About the job Experienced Recruiter of Externals Consultant

The client is seeking an Experienced Recruiter of Externals for a consultancy mission delivered by Keystone Solutions. As a Keystone Solutions consultant, you will represent our company on-site at the client, working within their Sourcing & Procurement department to facilitate the hiring and contracting of external Time & Material resources and monitor the end-to-end sourcing process.

Responsibilities


Support business managers in defining function descriptions that facilitate hiring the right profiles.
Safeguard qualitative sourcing by screening incoming CVs professionally and uniformly, formalizing screening results to ensure the correct quality/price balance.
Monitor open requests closely and decide with managers on next steps as needed.
Adhere to procurement, risk, and compliance guidelines.
Contribute to optimization of hiring processes and procedures.
Manage administrative activities linked to the hiring process.


Requirements


Master's degree or minimum 3 years, ideally 5 years, experience as Buyer/Recruiter.
At least 3-5 years of relevant experience.
Fluent usage of Microsoft Office tools (Outlook, Word, Excel).
Experience with workflow tools (such as Nétive platform) is preferable.
Negotiation skills.
Knowledge of the body-shopping sourcing market.
Commercial way of working with internal clients and suppliers.

Title: AI Engineer
Description:
I’m currently working with a major retail client in Brussels who are expanding their Azure Data Platform (ADP) team and are looking for a Freelance AI Engineer to help build and scale AI capabilities across their organisation.




This is a 12-month contract (with extension), requiring ~1 day per week onsite.




Role Overview

You’ll focus on developing AI platform enablers that support data engineers, data scientists, and product teams in delivering AI-driven use cases. The role involves:

Designing and implementing scalable, secure AI solutions on Azure & Databricks
Building reusable AI capabilities and accelerators
Defining best practices, governance, and architectural standards
Collaborating with cross-functional teams (data, platform, product)




Key Experience

3–7 years in AI/ML or cloud engineering roles
Strong experience with Azure (or AWS/GCP) and Databricks (Unity Catalog is a plus)
Hands-on experience integrating AI/ML into cloud platforms
Solid understanding of DevOps / CI-CD pipelines
Experience contributing to architecture and design decisions




Nice to Have

Python, GitHub (Actions), Terraform
Experience with Databricks AI features
Retail domain exposure




Details

Location: Brussels (hybrid – 1 day onsite)
Start: July/August
Duration: 12 months (extendable)
Language: English AND (French OR Dutch)

Title: Expert in AI/NLP/ML
HTML:
We currently have a vacancy for an Expert in AI/NLP/ML fluent in English, to offer his/her services as an expert who will be based in Brussels, Belgium. The work will be carried out either in the company's premises or on-site at the customer's premises. In the context of the first assignment, the successful candidate will be integrated into the Development team of the company that will closely cooperate with a major client's IT team on-site.

Tasks:

    • Engaging with Business stakeholders, organising workshops and meetings to gather requirements;
    • Analysing needs in the archiving domain and translating them into robust technical specifications;
    • Designing and proposing AI- and cloud-based architectures that are secure, scalable, and costeffective;
    • Developing, testing, and deploying solutions leveraging Large Language Models (LLMs), vector databases, and orchestration frameworks (e.g., LangChain);
    • Building prototypes, Proofs of Concept, and production-ready services using Python and Jupyter Notebooks;
    • Leveraging AWS Cloud Services (e.g., S3, Lambda, SageMaker, Bedrock, API Gateway, ECS/EKS, CloudFormation/Terraform);
    • Preparing and maintaining technical documentation, ensuring knowledge transfer within the team;
    • Integrating solutions into existing data and archiving systems;
    • Monitoring, optimising, and supporting deployed solutions over time;
    • Collaborating closely with Business, IT Infrastructure, and Security teams to ensure long-term sustainability.



Requirements

    • Master's degree in IT or a relevant discipline, combined with a minimum of 13 years of relevant working experience in IT;
    • At least 3 years of experience in LLMs: fine-tuning, prompting strategies, evaluation, and deployment;
    • At least 2 years of experience in LangChain or equivalent frameworks for LLM orchestration;
    • At least 2 years of experience in Vector databases;
    • At least 2 years of experience in Python, with practical use of Jupyter Notebooks;
    • At least 2 years of experience in AWS services for AI, data, and cloud-native development;
    • At least 1 year of experience in Machine Learning Operations, including CI/CD pipelines and monitoring of AI solutions;
    • At least 1 year of experience in data engineering and archiving best practices;
    • Good knowledge of Docker, Kubernetes and security best practices in AI and cloud-based deployments would be an advantage;
    • Knowledge of DevOps/Infrastructure-as-Code tools beyond AWS (Terraform, Ansible) and data governance frameworks and compliance standards (e.g., GDPR) would be an advantage;
    • AWS Solutions Architect, AWS Machine Learning, AWS DevOps Engineer, TensorFlow Developer or equivalent AI/ML credentials, Kubernetes Administrator (CKA), TOGAF or equivalent enterprise architecture framework certifications would be an asset;
    • Excellent command of the English language.


Benefits

If you are seeking a career in an exciting, dynamic and multicultural international environment with exciting opportunities that will boost your career, please send us your detailed CV in English, quoting reference (19007/09/2025).

We offer a competitive remuneration (either on contract basis or remuneration with full benefits package), based on qualifications and experience. All applications will be treated as confidential.

You may also consider all our other open vacancies by visiting the career section of our web site (www.eurodyn.com) and follow us on Twitter (@EURODYN_Careers) and LinkedIn.

EUROPEAN DYNAMICS (www.eurodyn.com) is a leading European Software, Information and Communication Technologies company, operating internationally (Athens, Brussels, Luxembourg, Copenhagen, Berlin, Stockholm, London, Nicosia, Valetta, Vienna, Den Haag, Hong Kong, etc.) The company employs over 1000 engineers, IT experts and consultants (around 3% PhD, 36% MSc and 53% BSc). We design and develop software applications using integrated, state-of-the-art technology. Our current IT projects have a value exceeding 300 million EURO. EUROPEAN DYNAMICS is a renowned supplier of IT services to European Union Institutions, international organizations, European Agencies and national government Administrations in 40 countries and 4 continents.

As part of our dedication to the diversity of our workforce, we are committed to Equal Employment Opportunity without regard for race, colour, age, national origin, ethnicity, gender, disability, sexual orientation, gender identity or religion.

EUROPEAN DYNAMICS (ED) adheres to the General Data Protection Regulation principles by applying its Privacy Policy as published in www.eurodyn.com/privacy. By submitting an application to this position and by sharing your personal data with ED, you acknowledge and accept its Policy and authorise ED to process your personal data for the purposes of the company's recruitment opportunities, in line to the Policy.

Title: Machine Learning Engineer
Description:
Artificialy is an AI company based in Switzerland, with offices in Lugano and Zurich. We design and deliver AI solutions that are impactful, transparent, and tailored to real-world industry needs.




Founded in 2020 by pioneers with over 40 years of combined experience in the field, our team brings together talented engineers, physicists, and mathematicians from across Europe. We are driven by curiosity, technical excellence, and a shared ambition to build AI systems that make a tangible difference.




We are looking for a Machine Learning Engineer, who will work with clients in the financial sector to bring AI solutions from prototype to production, with responsibilities spanning fine-tuning, deployment, inference optimization, monitoring, and continuous improvement. You will work in project teams alongside AI scientists, ensuring systems are reliable, scalable, and aligned with the high standards typical in banking.




This position can be based in Lugano or Zurich. You must be willing to work onsite at least 80% of the time. Candidates must be based in Switzerland or willing to relocate to Switzerland.




About the Role

Your responsibilities will span the full path from prototype to production - fine-tuning, deployment, inference optimization, monitoring, and continuous improvement of ML and LLM systems.
Projects may involve LLM serving and inference stacks, cost/latency optimization, structured-output reliability, model monitoring pipelines, or productionizing solutions designed alongside AI scientists.
You will work in project teams alongside AI scientists, software engineering, infrastructure, and domain experts to integrate solutions smoothly into enterprise ecosystems using modern deployment tooling (Docker, Kubernetes, CI/CD).




Qualifications and Skills

Master's degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or equivalent.
2+ years of working experience as ML Engineer or in a similar role
Experience deploying and operating ML/LLM systems in production (serving, monitoring, cost/latency optimization).
Strong programming skills (Python, SQL) and familiarity with deployment tooling (Docker, Kubernetes, CI/CD).
Hands-on experience with cloud platforms (AWS, Azure, or GCP).
Proficiency in English.




Nice to Have

Experience with LLM inference stacks (e.g., vLLM) and structured-output reliability.
Familiarity with Databricks or distributed data frameworks.
Experience in finance or other regulated industries.




We offer

Full-time permanent contract
Competitive compensation and growth opportunities
A stimulating scientific environment with an informal working atmosphere
Mentorship and continuous learning

Title: Data Scientist
Description:
Tasks & Responsibilities

Analytics & Solution Development

Develop innovative analytical solutions to address complex business challenges.
Explore large datasets to identify trends, opportunities, and actionable insights.
Apply statistical and AI-based techniques to support decision-making processes.

Business Collaboration

Work closely with technical teams and business stakeholders to understand requirements and translate them into analytical solutions.
Present findings and recommendations in a clear and understandable manner.
Support departments in identifying new opportunities to leverage data effectively.

Technology & Continuous Improvement

Contribute to the design, implementation, and enhancement of analytical applications.
Evaluate emerging technologies and recommend improvements to existing approaches.
Help establish best practices for analytics development and knowledge sharing within the team.




Must-Have

University degree in a quantitative discipline such as Computer Science, Mathematics, Statistics, Engineering, Data Analytics, or a related field.
Professional experience delivering analytical or AI-based solutions in a business environment.
Strong programming skills in Python.
Experience working with SQL and relational databases.
Knowledge of statistical analysis, machine learning techniques, or artificial intelligence concepts.
Experience using common Python libraries for analytics and data processing.
Strong analytical thinking and problem-solving skills.
Ability to communicate effectively with both technical and non-technical stakeholders.
Fluent German and good English.




Nice-to-Have

Experience working in technology-driven or regulated environments.
Exposure to cloud-based analytics platforms.
Familiarity with software development best practices and version control.
Interest in emerging AI technologies and their practical application.

Title: Stagiaire Data Scientist
Description:
CE QUE NOUS OFFRONS

Chez Cartier, vous intégrerez une communauté unie par un sens fort des responsabilités, où florissent connexion, collaboration et esprit collectif.

Nous valorisons l’individualité de nos talents, animés par un engagement commun envers l'excellence, inspirés par la diversité des cultures du monde, et attachés à respecter et enrichir notre héritage

unique. Nous sommes convaincus que des perspectives variées nourrissent l'innovation et nous encouragent à créer des expériences exceptionnelles.

Nous vous offrons un environnement stimulant et l’opportunité de contribuer, par votre vision personnelle, à un héritage de créativité.

Nous célébrons toutes les réussites, quelles qu’elles soient, favorisant ainsi des liens significatifs fondés sur la gratitude et le soutien mutuel et cultivant un fort sentiment d'appartenance.

Rejoignez-nous chez Cartier, un lieu unique et en constante évolution.

QUEL SERA VOTRE IMPACT ?

Missions

Au sein de l’équipe IA de la direction Transformation Digitale & Industrie 4.0, vous contribuez à accélérer le processus industriel à travers le développement et à la mise en place de nouveaux outils de transformation digitale et de développer des algorithmes ainsi que des solutions permettant de résoudre des problèmes complexes en mettant en œuvre des méthodologies d’Intelligence Artificielle.

Responsabilités

Au cours de votre stage, vos objectifs principaux seront les suivants :


Vous travaillez en collaboration avec les experts techniques et métiers pour comprendre la problématique de l’entreprise
En identifiant les besoins, les principaux défis pour proposer des solutions efficaces
En expliquant la méthodologie et en accompagnant à l’adoption des outils
En optimisant et sécurisant les processus dans le Manufacturing
En mettant en place des Dashboard de suivi
Vous extrayez, organisez et construisez les jeux de données nécessaires aux modélisations


En identifiant les sources de données précieuses
En traitant des données structurées et non structurées autour de designs 3D et des images issues de diverses bases (Windchill, SAP, BigQuery, …)
Vous construisez des modèles analytiques et prédictifs de « Machine Learning » ou de « Deep Learning » ou « GenAI » qui permettront de répondre à la problématique.


En développant des algorithmes et des solutions permettant de résoudre des problèmes complexes (Recommandation, Similarité, prédiction, ...)
Vous accompagnez la mise en production des modèles, des agents IA (MLOps)
En déployant des modèles scalables (Real-time vs Batch, Cloud)
En monitorant et en scorant les performances des modèles, agents déployés
En participant à la création des pipelines CI/CD et autres workflows

CE QUE NOUS RECHERCHONS


Formation en ingénierie, avec une orientation en informatique, mathématiques appliquées ou équivalent
Personne autonome, organisée et dotée d’un esprit analytique
Travail en équipe, force de proposition, aisance relationnelle
Maitrise des algorithmes


Votre Experience Avec Nous

Nous vous invitons à soumettre votre candidature complète directement sur notre site carrières. Si votre profil correspond à nos attentes, vous recevrez une invitation pour un entretien vidéo différé. Ce sera l'occasion de vous présenter, d'exprimer vos motivations et de partager vos attentes.

Notre équipe RH analysera ensuite votre entretien. Si votre candidature est retenue, vous recevrez, quelques jours avant le 1er octobre 2026, une invitation pour notre Talents Day, notre journée de recrutement 100% digitale. À cette occasion, une offre de stage spécifiquement adaptée à votre profil vous sera également proposée. #WeAreCartier#TalentDay

Cette journée immersive au cœur de l'univers Cartier vous permettra de rencontrer un(e) référent(e) RH et un(e) manager. Vous participerez à une étude de cas en groupe, suivie d'un entretien individuel.

Écrivez la prochaine étape de votre histoire avec Cartier.

Title: AI Scientist - LLM Systems

Artificialy is an AI company based in Switzerland, with offices in Lugano and Zurich. We design and deliver AI solutions that are impactful, transparent, and tailored to real-world industry needs.




Founded in 2020 by pioneers with over 40 years of combined experience in the field, our team brings together talented engineers, physicists, and mathematicians from across Europe. We are looking for a AI Scientist, who will work with clients in the financial sector to design and develop LLM-based solutions from early experimentation through to evaluation and delivery. You will own solution design and evaluation methodology within a project team, across a varied project portfolio.




This position can be based in Lugano or Zurich. You must be willing to work onsite at least 80% of the time. Candidates must be based in Switzerland or willing to relocate to Switzerland.




About the Role

Your responsibilities will span the full lifecycle of LLM-based initiatives, from early experimentation and solution design through evaluation, delivery, and continuous improvement.
Projects may involve retrieval-augmented generation, agentic systems, fine-tuning, LLM-based evaluation frameworks (benchmarks, LLM-as-judge, error analysis), or other applied generative-AI solutions across a varied project portfolio.
You will own solution design and evaluation methodology within project teams, collaborating with software engineering, infrastructure, and domain experts to integrate solutions into enterprise ecosystems and present results to technical and non-technical stakeholders alike.




Qualifications and Skills

Master's degree or PhD in Computer Science, Mathematics, Engineering, or equivalent.
5+ years of working experience as AI Scientist or in a similar role or PhD
Hands-on experience building LLM-based systems (RAG, agents, fine-tuning, or similar).
Experience designing evaluations for ML/LLM systems (benchmarks, LLM-as-judge, error analysis).
Strong programming skills (Python, SQL) and familiarity with ML libraries (TensorFlow, PyTorch, Scikit-learn, etc.).
Strong communication and presentation skills to technical and non-technical stakeholders.
Proficiency in English.




Nice to Have

Experience in finance or other regulated industries.
Journal publications
Hands-on experience with cloud platforms (Azure preferred).

HTML:
Artificialy is an AI company based in Switzerland, with offices in Lugano and Zurich. We design and deliver AI solutions that are impactful, transparent, and tailored to real-world industry needs.


Founded in 2020 by pioneers with over 40 years of combined experience in the field, our team brings together talented engineers, physicists, and mathematicians from across Europe. We are driven by curiosity, technical excellence, and a shared ambition to build AI systems that make a tangible difference.


We are looking for a AI Scientist, who will work with clients in the financial sector to design and develop LLM-based solutions from early experimentation through to evaluation and delivery. You will own solution design and evaluation methodology within a project team, across a varied project portfolio.


This position can be based in Lugano or Zurich. You must be willing to work onsite at least 80% of the time. Candidates must be based in Switzerland or willing to relocate to Switzerland.


About the Role

  • Your responsibilities will span the full lifecycle of LLM-based initiatives, from early experimentation and solution design through evaluation, delivery, and continuous improvement.
  • Projects may involve retrieval-augmented generation, agentic systems, fine-tuning, LLM-based evaluation frameworks (benchmarks, LLM-as-judge, error analysis), or other applied generative-AI solutions across a varied project portfolio.
  • You will own solution design and evaluation methodology within project teams, collaborating with software engineering, infrastructure, and domain experts to integrate solutions into enterprise ecosystems and present results to technical and non-technical stakeholders alike.


Qualifications and Skills

  • Master's degree or PhD in Computer Science, Mathematics, Engineering, or equivalent.
  • 5+ years of working experience as AI Scientist or in a similar role or PhD
  • Hands-on experience building LLM-based systems (RAG, agents, fine-tuning, or similar).
  • Experience designing evaluations for ML/LLM systems (benchmarks, LLM-as-judge, error analysis).
  • Strong programming skills (Python, SQL) and familiarity with ML libraries (TensorFlow, PyTorch, Scikit-learn, etc.).
  • Strong communication and presentation skills to technical and non-technical stakeholders.
  • Proficiency in English.


Nice to Have

  • Experience in finance or other regulated industries.
  • Journal publications
  • Hands-on experience with cloud platforms (Azure preferred).


We offer

  • Full-time permanent contract
  • Competitive compensation and growth opportunities
  • A stimulating scientific environment with an informal working atmosphere
  • Mentorship and continuous learning

Title: Software Engineer
Description:
About Healio




Healio is a startup building the next generation of workforce planning software for healthcare institutions. Hospitals, clinics and care institutions are under massive pressure: staff shortages, complex schedules, high absenteeism, and growing dissatisfaction among healthcare teams. We believe planning should not be a painful top-down administrative process, but a collaborative, intelligent and fair system that empowers both managers and caregivers.




We are an early-stage Swiss startup based in Lausanne, founded by an experienced team with deep healthcare, product and startup experience, including previous company-building and exit experience. We already work closely with healthcare institutions and are building a product directly with users in the field.




We are looking for an ambitious Software Engineer to join us as an early team member and help shape the product, the technology and the company from the ground up.




The role




As a Software Engineer at Healio, you will work closely with the founders and CTO to build, improve and scale our platform.




You will contribute across the stack, from user-facing features to backend logic, integrations, data flows and AI-enabled product experiences. This is not a narrow execution role. At this stage, every system in the company is still being designed, and you'll have a real say in the decisions to grow and scale the company. We are looking for someone who wants to understand the product, the users, the technical architecture and the business.




We are AI-native in practice: we constantly evolve how humans and AI agents build software together. You will help define what professional engineering looks like when AI is central to the development of products, which makes fundamentals (architecture, testing, review, security) more important, not less.




What you'll do




Build across a modern TypeScript stack: a React Router full-stack web app, a React Native app for caregivers, a multi-tenant PostgreSQL, and infrastructure as code.
Work on the hard core of the product: scheduling and planning algorithms, including a constraint-solver optimisation service. Real computer science, not just CRUD.
Design clean, scalable and maintainable product features.
Integrate AI tools and AI-powered features into the product and the development workflow.
Build software that must be trustworthy: multi-tenant isolation, passkey authentication, automated security scanning, and a serious testing culture.
Own cyber security as a first-class concern: with sensitive health data, threat modeling, defense in depth and secure-by-default design are everyday engineering, not an afterthought.
Collaborate with founders, product and users to turn real healthcare problems into elegant software.
Contribute to technical decisions, architecture, code quality and engineering culture.
Help us move fast while building a product healthcare institutions can trust.




Who we are looking for




We are looking for someone who is technically strong, curious, pragmatic and motivated by the opportunity to build something meaningful from an early stage.




Ideally, you have:




Strong experience with TypeScript.
Experience with React and modern full-stack web development.
A good understanding of APIs, databases, authentication and scalable software architecture.
You already work with AI coding tools daily and have developed judgment about when to trust them. You know how systems work end to end, data modeling, software design patterns, security and are able to verify and own everything we ship.
Strong written communication: in a small team working with AI agents and real healthcare customers, clearly specifying a problem is half the job.
A product mindset: you care about what users need, not just what the code does.
The ability to work independently, take ownership and move fast.
Good communication skills in English. French and/or German are a plus.
A long-term mindset and the desire to grow with the company.




You don't need to tick every box. What matters is that you are smart, reliable, and have both the AI-era reflexes and the engineering foundations to build software that healthcare institutions can trust.




Why join Healio?




This is a rare opportunity to join a startup at a very early stage, with real market traction, strong founder experience and a meaningful problem to solve.




As an early employee, you will have:




The chance to shape a company where almost nothing is fixed yet: you won't just influence the codebase, you'll help define how we do architecture, design, operations, even how we sell.
Direct collaboration with experienced founders.
Exposure to real customers and real healthcare workflows.
A steep learning curve in product, engineering, AI and startup building.
Flexible working conditions and home office.
Offices in Lausanne.
A multilingual environment across French, English and German.
The opportunity to receive an attractive equity / share package as an early team member.




We are not looking for someone who just wants a job. We are looking for someone who wants to build, learn, take ownership and be part of the long-term journey.




How to apply




Send your CV, LinkedIn or GitHub profile to:



apply@healio.ch




Include a few lines about why Healio interests you and what kind of products you like to build. Feel free to include anything that shows how you build today or what you've shipped.

Title: Data Scientist - Remote
Description:
Job Title: Data Scientist

Job Type: Contractor

Location: Remote

Job Summary

In this role, you'll apply your expertise to help train next-generation AI systems. Your work will shape how models learn, reason, and perform through high-quality, real-world input. No prior experience in AI is required — your domain knowledge is what matters.

Key Responsibilities


Collect, clean, and preprocess diverse data sets to ensure integrity and readiness for analysis.
Develop, validate, and implement statistical models to extract actionable insights from complex data.
Conduct exploratory data analysis to identify trends, patterns, and opportunities for business improvement.
Present findings and recommendations through compelling data visualizations and reports, tailored for technical and non-technical stakeholders.
Collaborate closely with team members to design and execute end-to-end data projects, from ideation to delivery.
Continuously improve analytical methodologies and automation processes to enhance data workflows.
Communicate effectively in both written and verbal formats, ensuring clarity and impact of data-driven recommendations.


Required Skills And Qualifications


Expertise in statistics, mathematics, and data analysis techniques.
Proven experience in collecting, handling, and cleaning large, complex data sets.
Proficiency in programming languages such as Python, R, or similar for data manipulation and modeling.
Strong data modeling skills with experience in building and validating predictive models.
Advanced ability to visualize data using tools like Tableau, Power BI, or related libraries.
Meticulous attention to detail and commitment to data quality at every stage.
Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with an emphasis on clarity and precision.


Preferred Qualifications


Experience working in a remote, cross-functional, or customer-focused team environment.
Background in developing and deploying machine learning solutions in production settings.
